Genesis1:1 In the beginning
Genesis1:2 the earth was formless and desolate. The raging ocean that covered everything was engulfed in total darkness

Genesis2:4 And that is how the universe was created. When the Lord God made the universe
Genesis2:5 there were no plants on the earth and no seeds had sprouted
Genesis2:6 but water would come up from beneath the surface and water the ground.
Genesis2:7 Then the Lord God took some soil from the ground and formed a man out of it; he breathed life-giving breath into his nostrils and the man began to live.
Genesis2:8 Then the Lord God planted a garden in Eden
Genesis2:9 He made all kinds of beautiful trees grow there and produce good fruit. In the middle of the garden stood the tree that gives life and the tree that gives knowledge of what is good and what is bad.
Genesis2:10 A stream flowed in Eden and watered the garden; beyond Eden it divided into four rivers.
Genesis2:11 The first river is the Pishon; it flows around the country of Havilah. (
Genesis2:12 Pure gold is found there and also rare perfume and precious stones.)
Genesis2:13 The second river is the Gihon; it flows around the country of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The third river is the Tigris

Genesis8:12 Then he waited another seven days and sent out the dove once more; this time it did not come back.

Exodus26:6 Make fifty gold hooks with which to join the two sets into one piece.

Exodus26:8 Make them all the same size
Exodus26:9 Sew five of them together in one set
Exodus26:10 Put fifty loops on the edge of the last piece of one set
Exodus26:11 Make fifty bronze hooks and put them in the loops to join the two sets so as to form one cover.
Exodus26:12 Hang the extra half piece over the back of the Tent.
Exodus26:13 The extra half yard on each side of the length is to hang over the sides of the Tent to cover it.
Exodus26:14 “Make two more coverings
Exodus26:15 “Make upright frames for the Tent out of acacia wood.
Exodus26:16 Each frame is to be 15 feet long and 27 inches wide
Exodus26:17 with two matching projections
Exodus26:18 Make twenty frames for the south side
Exodus26:19 and forty silver bases to go under them
Exodus26:20 Make twenty frames for the north side of the Tent
Exodus26:21 and forty silver bases
Exodus26:22 For the back of the Tent on the west
Exodus26:23 and two frames for the corners.
Exodus26:24 These corner frames are to be joined at the bottom and connected all the way to the top. The two frames that form the two corners are to be made in this way.
Exodus26:25 So there will be eight frames with their sixteen silver bases
Exodus26:26 “Make fifteen crossbars of acacia wood
Exodus26:27 five for the frames on the other side
Exodus26:28 The middle crossbar
Exodus26:29 Cover the frames with gold and fit them with gold rings to hold the crossbars
Exodus26:30 Set up the Tent according to the plan that I showed you on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 “Make a curtain of fine linen woven with blue
Exodus26:32 Hang it on four posts of acacia wood covered with gold
Exodus26:33 Place the curtain under the row of hooks in the roof of the Tent
Exodus26:34 Put the lid on the Covenant Box.
Exodus26:35 Outside the Most Holy Place put the table against the north side of the Tent and the lampstand against the south side.
Exodus26:36 “For the entrance of the Tent make a curtain of fine linen woven with blue
Exodus26:37 For this curtain make five posts of acacia wood covered with gold and fitted with gold hooks; make five bronze bases for these posts.
Exodus27:1 “Make an altar out of acacia wood. It is to be square
Exodus27:2 Make projections at the top of the four corners. They are to form one piece with the altar
Exodus27:3 Make pans for the greasy ashes
Exodus27:4 Make a bronze grating and put four bronze carrying rings on its corners.
Exodus27:5 Put the grating under the rim of the altar
Exodus27:6 Make carrying poles of acacia wood
Exodus27:7 and put them in the rings on each side of the altar when it is carried.
Exodus27:8 Make the altar out of boards and leave it hollow
Exodus27:9 “For the Tent of my presence make an enclosure out of fine linen curtains. On the south side the curtains are to be 50 yards long
Exodus27:10 supported by twenty bronze posts in twenty bronze bases
Exodus27:11 Do the same on the north side of the enclosure.
Exodus27:12 On the west side there are to be curtains 25 yards long
Exodus27:13 On the east side
Exodus27:14 On each side of the entrance there are to be 7½ yards of curtains
Exodus27:15 
Exodus27:16 For the entrance itself there is to be a curtain 10 yards long made of fine linen woven with blue
Exodus27:17 All the posts around the enclosure are to be connected with silver rods
Exodus27:18 The enclosure is to be 50 yards long
Exodus27:19 All the equipment that is used in the Tent and all the pegs for the Tent and for the enclosure are to be made of bronze.
Exodus27:20 “Command the people of Israel to bring you the best olive oil for the lamp
Exodus27:21 Aaron and his sons are to set up the lamp in the Tent of my presence outside the curtain which is in front of the Covenant Box. There in my presence it is to burn from evening until morning. This command is to be kept forever by the Israelites and their descendants.
Exodus28:1 “Summon your brother Aaron and his sons
Exodus28:2 Make priestly garments for your brother Aaron
Exodus28:3 Call all the skilled workers to whom I have given ability
Exodus28:4 Tell them to make a breastpiece
Exodus28:5 The skilled workers are to use blue
Exodus28:6 “They are to make the ephod of blue
Exodus28:7 Two shoulder straps

Exodus28:10 in the order of their birth

Exodus28:12 Put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod to represent the twelve tribes of Israel. In this way Aaron will carry their names on his shoulders
Exodus28:13 Make two gold settings
Exodus28:14 and two chains of pure gold twisted like cords
Exodus28:15 “Make a breastpiece for the High Priest to use in determining God's will. It is to be made of the same materials as the ephod and with similar embroidery.
Exodus28:16 It is to be square and folded double
Exodus28:17 Mount four rows of precious stones on it; in the first row mount a ruby
Exodus28:18 in the second row
Exodus28:19 in the third row
Exodus28:20 and in the fourth row

Exodus28:24 and fasten the two gold cords to the two rings.
Exodus28:25 Fasten the other two ends of the cords to the two settings
Exodus28:26 Then make two rings of gold and attach them to the lower corners of the breastpiece on the inside edge next to the ephod.
Exodus28:27 Make two more gold rings and attach them to the lower part of the front of the two shoulder straps of the ephod
Exodus28:28 Tie the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord

Exodus28:34

Exodus28:37 Tie it to the front of the turban with a blue cord.
Exodus28:38 Aaron is to wear it on his forehead

Exodus29:1 “This is what you are to do to Aaron and his sons to dedicate them as priests in my service. Take one young bull and two rams without any defects.
Exodus29:2 Use the best wheat flour
Exodus29:3 Put them in a basket and offer them to me when you sacrifice the bull and the two rams.
Exodus29:4 “Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent of my presence
Exodus29:5 Then dress Aaron in the priestly garments—the shirt
Exodus29:6 Put the turban on him and tie on it the sacred sign of dedication engraved ‘Dedicated to the Lord.’
Exodus29:7 Then take the anointing oil
Exodus29:8 “Bring his sons and put shirts on them;
Exodus29:9 put sashes around their waists and tie caps on their heads. That is how you are to ordain Aaron and his sons. They and their descendants are to serve me as priests forever.
Exodus29:10 “Bring the bull to the front of the Tent of my presence and tell Aaron and his sons to put their hands on its head.
Exodus29:11 Kill the bull there in my holy presence at the entrance of the Tent.
Exodus29:12 Take some of the bull's blood and with your finger put it on the projections of the altar. Then pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
Exodus29:13 Next
Exodus29:14 But burn the bull's flesh
Exodus29:15 “Take one of the rams and tell Aaron and his sons to put their hands on its head.
Exodus29:16 Kill it
Exodus29:17 Cut the ram in pieces; wash its internal organs and its hind legs
Exodus29:18 Burn the whole ram on the altar as a food offering. The odor of this offering pleases me.
Exodus29:19 “Take the other ram—the ram used for dedication—and tell Aaron and his sons to put their hands on its head.
Exodus29:20 Kill it
Exodus29:21 Take some of the blood that is on the altar and some of the anointing oil
Exodus29:22 “Cut away the ram's fat
Exodus29:23 From the basket of bread which has been offered to me
Exodus29:24 Put all this food in the hands of Aaron and his sons and have them dedicate it to me as a special gift.
Exodus29:25 Then take it from them and burn it on the altar
Exodus29:26 “Take the breast of this ram and dedicate it to me as a special gift. This part of the animal will be yours.
Exodus29:27 “When a priest is ordained
Exodus29:28 It is my unchanging decision that when my people make their fellowship offerings
Exodus29:29 “Aaron's priestly garments are to be handed on to his sons after his death
Exodus29:30 The son of Aaron who succeeds him as priest and who goes into the Tent of my presence to serve in the Holy Place is to wear these garments for seven days.
Exodus29:31 “Take the meat of the ram used for the ordination of Aaron and his sons and boil it in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 At the entrance of the Tent of my presence they are to eat it along with the bread left in the basket.
Exodus29:33 They shall eat what was used in the ritual of forgiveness at their ordination. Only priests may eat this food
Exodus29:34 If some of the meat or some of the bread is not eaten by morning
Exodus29:35 “Perform the rites of ordination for Aaron and his sons for seven days exactly as I have commanded you.
Exodus29:36 Each day you must offer a bull as a sacrifice
Exodus29:37 Do this every day for seven days. Then the altar will be completely holy
Exodus29:38 “Every day for all time to come
Exodus29:39 Sacrifice one of the lambs in the morning and the other in the evening.
Exodus29:40 With the first lamb offer two pounds of fine wheat flour mixed with one quart of pure olive oil. Pour out one quart of wine as an offering.
Exodus29:41 Sacrifice the second lamb in the evening
Exodus29:42 For all time to come
Exodus29:43 There I will meet the people of Israel
Exodus29:44 I will make the Tent and the altar holy
Exodus29:45 I will live among the people of Israel
Exodus29:46 They will know that I am the Lord their God who brought them out of Egypt so that I could live among them. I am the Lord their God.

Exodus38:7 and put them in the rings on each side of the altar. The altar was made of boards and was hollow.
Exodus38:8 He made the bronze basin and its bronze base out of the mirrors belonging to the women who served at the entrance of the Tent of the Lord 's presence.
Exodus38:9 For the Tent of the Lord 's presence he made the enclosure out of fine linen curtains. On the south side the curtains were 50 yards long
Exodus38:10 supported by twenty bronze posts in twenty bronze bases
Exodus38:11 The enclosure was the same on the north side.
Exodus38:12 On the west side there were curtains 25 yards long
Exodus38:13 On the east side
Exodus38:14 On each side of the entrance there were 7½ yards of curtains
Exodus38:15 
Exodus38:16 All the curtains around the enclosure were made of fine linen.
Exodus38:17 The bases for the posts were made of bronze
Exodus38:18 The curtain for the entrance of the enclosure was made of fine linen woven with blue
Exodus38:19 It was supported by four posts in four bronze bases. Their hooks
Exodus38:20 All the pegs for the Tent and for the surrounding enclosure were made of bronze.
Exodus38:21 Here is a list of the amounts of the metals used in the Tent of the Lord 's presence
Exodus38:22 Bezalel
Exodus38:23 His helper
Exodus38:24 All the gold that had been dedicated to the Lord for the sacred Tent weighed 2
Exodus38:25 The silver from the census of the community weighed 7
Exodus38:26 This amount equaled the total paid by all persons enrolled in the census
Exodus38:27 Of the silver
Exodus38:28 With the remaining 50 pounds of silver Bezalel made the rods
Exodus38:29 The bronze which was dedicated to the Lord amounted to 5
Exodus38:30 With it he made the bases for the entrance of the Tent of the Lord 's presence
Exodus38:31 the bases for the surrounding enclosure and for the entrance of the enclosure
Exodus39:1 With the blue
Exodus39:2 They made the ephod of fine linen; blue
Exodus39:3 They hammered out sheets of gold and cut them into thin strips to be worked into the fine linen and into the blue
Exodus39:4 They made two shoulder straps for the ephod and attached them to its sides
Exodus39:5 The finely woven belt
Exodus39:6 They prepared the carnelians and mounted them in gold settings; they were skillfully engraved with the names of the twelve sons of Jacob.
Exodus39:7 They put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod to represent the twelve tribes of Israel
Exodus39:8 They made the breastpiece of the same materials as the ephod and with similar embroidery.
Exodus39:9 It was square and folded double
Exodus39:10 They mounted four rows of precious stones on it: in the first row they mounted a ruby
Exodus39:11 in the second row
Exodus39:12 in the third row
Exodus39:13 and in the fourth row
Exodus39:14 Each of the twelve stones had engraved on it the name of one of the sons of Jacob
Exodus39:15 For the breastpiece they made chains of pure gold
Exodus39:16 They made two gold settings and two gold rings and attached the two rings to the upper corners of the breastpiece.
Exodus39:17 They fastened the two gold cords to the two rings
Exodus39:18 and fastened the other two ends of the cords to the two settings and in this way attached them in front to the shoulder straps of the ephod.
Exodus39:19 They made two rings of gold and attached them to the lower corners of the breastpiece
Exodus39:20 They made two more gold rings and attached them to the lower part of the front of the two shoulder straps of the ephod
Exodus39:21 Just as the Lord had commanded Moses
Exodus39:22 The robe that goes under the ephod was made entirely of blue wool.
Exodus39:23 The hole for the head was reinforced with a woven binding to keep it from tearing.
Exodus39:24 All around its lower hem they put pomegranates of fine linen and of blue
Exodus39:25 
Exodus39:26 
Exodus39:27 They made the shirts for Aaron and his sons
Exodus39:28 and the turban
Exodus39:29 and the sash of fine linen and of blue
Exodus39:30 They made the ornament
Exodus39:31 They tied it to the front of the turban with a blue cord
Exodus39:32 All the work on the Tent of the Lord 's presence was finally completed. The Israelites made everything just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:33 They brought to Moses the Tent and all its equipment
Exodus39:34 the covering of rams' skin dyed red; the covering of fine leather; the curtain;
Exodus39:35 the Covenant Box containing the stone tablets
Exodus39:36 the table and all its equipment
Exodus39:37 the lampstand of pure gold
Exodus39:38 the gold altar; the anointing oil; the sweet-smelling incense; the curtain for the entrance of the Tent;
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar with its bronze grating
Exodus39:40 the curtains for the enclosure and its posts and bases; the curtain for the entrance of the enclosure and its ropes; the Tent pegs; all the equipment to be used in the Tent;
Exodus39:41 and the magnificent garments the priests were to wear in the Holy Place—the sacred clothes for Aaron the priest and for his sons.
Exodus39:42 The Israelites had done all the work just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:43 Moses examined everything and saw that they had made it all just as the Lord had commanded. So Moses blessed them.
Exodus40:1 The Lord said to Moses
Exodus40:2 “On the first day of the first month set up the Tent of the Lord 's presence.
Exodus40:3 Place in it the Covenant Box containing the Ten Commandments and put the curtain in front of it.
Exodus40:4 Bring in the table and place the equipment on it. Also bring in the lampstand and set up the lamps on it.
Exodus40:5 Put the gold altar for burning incense in front of the Covenant Box and hang the curtain at the entrance of the Tent.
Exodus40:6 Put in front of the Tent the altar for burning offerings.
Exodus40:7 Put the washbasin between the Tent and the altar and fill it with water.
Exodus40:8 Put up the surrounding enclosure and hang the curtain at its entrance.
Exodus40:9 “Then dedicate the Tent and all its equipment by anointing it with the sacred oil
Exodus40:10 Next
Exodus40:11 Also dedicate the washbasin and its base in the same way.
Exodus40:12 “Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent
Exodus40:13 Dress Aaron in the priestly garments
Exodus40:14 Bring his sons and put the shirts on them.
Exodus40:15 Then anoint them
Exodus40:16 Moses did everything just as the Lord had commanded.
Exodus40:17 So on the first day of the first month of the second year after they left Egypt
Exodus40:18 Moses put down its bases
Exodus40:19 He spread out the covering over the Tent and put the outer covering over it
Exodus40:20 Then he took the two stone tablets and put them in the Covenant Box. He put the poles in the rings of the Box and put the lid on it.
Exodus40:21 Then he put the Box in the Tent and hung up the curtain. In this way he screened off the Covenant Box
Exodus40:22 He put the table in the Tent
Exodus40:23 and placed on it the bread offered to the Lord
Exodus40:24 He put the lampstand in the Tent
Exodus40:25 and there in the Lord 's presence he lit the lamps
Exodus40:26 He put the gold altar in the Tent
Exodus40:27 and burned the sweet-smelling incense
Exodus40:28 He hung the curtain at the entrance of the Tent
Exodus40:29 and there in front of the curtain he placed the altar for burning offerings. On it he sacrificed the burnt offering and the grain offering
Exodus40:30 He put the washbasin between the Tent and the altar and filled it with water.
Exodus40:31 Moses
Exodus40:32 whenever they went into the Tent or to the altar
Exodus40:33 Moses set up the enclosure around the Tent and the altar and hung the curtain at the entrance of the enclosure. So he finished all the work.
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the Tent and the dazzling light of the Lord 's presence filled it.
Exodus40:35 Because of this
Exodus40:36 The Israelites moved their camp to another place only when the cloud lifted from the Tent.
Exodus40:37 As long as the cloud stayed there
Exodus40:38 During all their wanderings they could see the cloud of the Lord 's presence over the Tent during the day and a fire burning above it during the night.
Leviticus1:1 The Lord called to Moses from the Tent of the Lord 's presence and gave him the following rules
Leviticus1:2 for the Israelites to observe when they offer their sacrifices. When you offer an animal sacrifice
Leviticus1:3 If you are offering one of your cattle as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:4 You shall put your hand on its head
Leviticus1:5 You shall kill the bull there
Leviticus1:6 Then you shall skin the animal and cut it up
Leviticus1:7 and the priests shall arrange firewood on the altar and light it.
Leviticus1:8 They shall put on the fire the pieces of the animal
Leviticus1:9 You must wash the internal organs and the hind legs
Leviticus1:10 If you are offering one of your sheep or goats
Leviticus1:11 You shall kill it on the north side of the altar
Leviticus1:12 After you cut it up
Leviticus1:13 You must wash the internal organs and the hind legs
Leviticus1:14 If you are offering a bird as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:15 The priest shall present it at the altar
Leviticus1:16 He shall remove the crop and its contents and throw them away on the east side of the altar where the ashes are put.
Leviticus1:17 He shall take hold of its wings and tear its body open
Leviticus2:1 When any of you present an offering of grain to the Lord
Leviticus2:2 and bring it to the Aaronite priests. The officiating priest shall take a handful of the flour and oil and all of the incense and burn it on the altar as a token that it has all been offered to the Lord. The odor of this food offering is pleasing to the Lord.
Leviticus2:3 The rest of the grain offering belongs to the priests; it is very holy
Leviticus2:4 If the offering is bread baked in an oven
Leviticus2:5 If the offering is bread cooked on a griddle
Leviticus2:6 Crumble it up and pour the oil on it when you present it as an offering.
Leviticus2:7 If the offering is bread cooked in a pan
Leviticus2:8 Bring it as an offering to the Lord and present it to the priest
Leviticus2:9 The priest will take part of it as a token that it has all been offered to the Lord
Leviticus2:10 The rest of the offering belongs to the priests; it is very holy
Leviticus2:11 None of the grain offerings which you present to the Lord may be made with yeast; you must never use yeast or honey in food offered to the Lord.
Leviticus2:12 An offering of the first grain that you harvest each year shall be brought to the Lord
Leviticus2:13 Put salt on every grain offering
Leviticus2:14 When you bring to the Lord an offering of the first grain harvested
Leviticus2:15 Add olive oil and put incense on it.
Leviticus2:16 The priest will burn that part of the meal and oil that is to serve as a token
Leviticus3:1 When any of you offer one of your cattle as a fellowship offering
Leviticus3:2 You shall put your hand on the head of the animal and kill it at the entrance of the Tent of the Lord 's presence. The Aaronite priests shall throw the blood against all four sides of the altar
Leviticus3:3 and present the following parts of the animal as a food offering to the Lord: all the fat on the internal organs
Leviticus3:4 the kidneys and the fat on them
Leviticus3:5 The priests shall burn all this on the altar along with the burnt offerings. The odor of this food offering is pleasing to the Lord.
Leviticus3:6 If a sheep or goat is used as a fellowship offering
Leviticus3:7 If you offer a sheep
Leviticus3:8 you shall put your hand on its head and kill it in front of the Tent. The priests shall throw its blood against all four sides of the altar
Leviticus3:9 and present the following parts of the animal as a food offering to the Lord: the fat
Leviticus3:10 the kidneys and the fat on them
Leviticus3:11 The officiating priest shall burn all this on the altar as a food offering to the Lord.
Leviticus3:12 If you offer a goat
Leviticus3:13 you shall put your hand on its head and kill it in front of the Tent. The priests shall throw its blood against all four sides of the altar
Leviticus3:14 and present the following parts as a food offering to the Lord: all the fat on the internal organs
Leviticus3:15 the kidneys and the fat on them
Leviticus3:16 The priest shall burn all this on the altar as a food offering pleasing to the Lord. All the fat belongs to the Lord.
Leviticus3:17 No Israelite may eat any fat or any blood; this is a rule to be kept forever by all Israelites wherever they live.
Leviticus4:1 The Lord commanded Moses
Leviticus4:2 to tell the people of Israel that anyone who sinned and broke any of the Lord 's commands without intending to
Leviticus4:3 If it is the High Priest who sins and so brings guilt on the people
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring the bull to the entrance of the Tent
Leviticus4:5 Then the High Priest shall take some of the bull's blood and carry it into the Tent.
Leviticus4:6 He shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it in front of the sacred curtain seven times.
Leviticus4:7 Then he shall put some of the blood on the projections at the corners of the incense altar in the Tent. He shall pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar used for burning sacrifices
Leviticus4:8 From this bull he shall take all the fat
Leviticus4:9 the kidneys and the fat on them
Leviticus4:10 The priest shall take this fat and burn it on the altar used for the burnt offerings
Leviticus4:11 But he shall take its skin
Leviticus4:12 carry it all outside the camp to the ritually clean place where the ashes are poured out
Leviticus4:13 If it is the whole community of Israel that sins and becomes guilty of breaking one of the Lord 's commands without intending to
Leviticus4:14 then as soon as the sin becomes known
Leviticus4:15 the leaders of the community shall put their hands on its head
Leviticus4:16 The High Priest shall take some of the bull's blood into the Tent
Leviticus4:17 dip his finger in it
Leviticus4:18 He shall put some of the blood on the projections at the corners of the incense altar inside the Tent and pour out the rest of it at the base of the altar used for burning sacrifices
Leviticus4:19 Then he shall take all its fat and burn it on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He shall do the same thing with this bull as he does with the bull for the sin offering
Leviticus4:21 Then he shall take the bull outside the camp and burn it
Leviticus4:22 If it is a ruler who sins and becomes guilty of breaking one of the Lord 's commands without intending to
Leviticus4:23 then as soon as the sin is called to his attention
Leviticus4:24 He shall put his hand on its head and kill it on the north side of the altar
Leviticus4:25 The priest shall dip his finger in the blood of the animal
Leviticus4:26 Then he shall burn all of its fat on the altar
Leviticus4:27 If any of you people sin and become guilty of breaking one of the Lord 's commands without intending to
Leviticus4:28 then as soon as the sin is called to your attention
Leviticus4:29 You shall put your hand on its head and kill it on the north side of the altar
Leviticus4:30 The priest shall dip his finger in the blood of the animal
Leviticus4:31 Then he shall remove all its fat
Leviticus4:32 If you bring a sheep as a sin offering
Leviticus4:33 You shall put your hand on its head and kill it on the north side of the altar
Leviticus4:34 The priest shall dip his finger in the blood of the animal
Leviticus4:35 Then he shall remove all its fat
Leviticus5:1 Sin offerings are required in the following cases. If you are officially summoned to give evidence in court and do not give information about something you have seen or heard
Leviticus5:2 If you unintentionally touch anything ritually unclean
Leviticus5:3 If you unintentionally touch anything of human origin that is unclean
Leviticus5:4 If you make a careless vow
Leviticus5:5 When you are guilty
Leviticus5:6 and as the penalty for your sin you must bring to the Lord a female sheep or goat as an offering. The priest shall offer the sacrifice for your sin.
Leviticus5:7 If you cannot afford a sheep or a goat
Leviticus5:8 You shall bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 and sprinkle some of its blood against the side of the altar. The rest of the blood will be drained out at the base of the altar. This is an offering to take away sin.
Leviticus5:10 Then he shall offer the second bird as a burnt offering
Leviticus5:11 If you cannot afford two doves or two pigeons
Leviticus5:12 You shall bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 In this way the priest shall offer the sacrifice for your sin
Leviticus5:14 The Lord gave the following regulations to Moses.
Leviticus5:15 If any of you sin unintentionally by failing to hand over the payments that are sacred to the Lord
Leviticus5:16 You must make the payments you have failed to hand over and must pay an additional 20 percent. You shall give it to the priest
Leviticus5:17 If any of you sin unintentionally by breaking any of the Lord 's commands
Leviticus5:18 You must bring to the priest as a repayment offering a male sheep or goat without any defects. Its value is to be determined according to the official standard. The priest shall offer the sacrifice for the sin which you committed unintentionally
Leviticus5:19 It is a repayment offering for the sin you committed against the Lord.
Leviticus6:1 The Lord gave the following regulations to Moses.
Leviticus6:2 An offering is to be made if any of you sin against the Lord by refusing to return what another Israelite has left as a deposit or by stealing something from him or by cheating him
Leviticus6:3 or by lying about something that has been lost and swearing that you did not find it.
Leviticus6:4 When you sin in any of these ways
Leviticus6:5 
Leviticus6:6 You shall bring to the priest as your repayment offering to the Lord a male sheep or goat without any defects. Its value is to be determined according to the official standard.
Leviticus6:7 The priest shall offer the sacrifice for your sin
Leviticus6:8 The Lord commanded Moses
Leviticus6:9 to give Aaron and his sons the following regulations for burnt offerings. A burnt offering is to be left on the altar all night long
Leviticus6:10 Then the priest
Leviticus6:11 Then he shall change his clothes and take the ashes outside the camp to a ritually clean place.
Leviticus6:12 The fire on the altar must be kept burning and never allowed to go out. Every morning the priest shall put firewood on it
Leviticus6:13 The fire must always be kept burning on the altar and never allowed to go out.
Leviticus6:14 The following are the regulations for grain offerings. An Aaronite priest shall present the grain offering to the Lord in front of the altar.
Leviticus6:15 Then he shall take a handful of the flour and oil
Leviticus6:16 The priests shall eat the rest of it. It shall be made into bread baked without yeast and eaten in a holy place
Leviticus6:17 
Leviticus6:18 For all time to come any of the male descendants of Aaron may eat it as their continuing share of the food offered to the Lord. Anyone else who touches a food offering will be harmed by the power of its holiness.
Leviticus6:19 The Lord gave Moses the following regulations
Leviticus6:20 for the ordination of an Aaronite priest. On the day he is ordained
Leviticus6:21 It is to be mixed with oil and cooked on a griddle and then crumbled and presented as a grain offering
Leviticus6:22 For all time to come this offering is to be made by every descendant of Aaron who is serving as High Priest. It shall be completely burned as a sacrifice to the Lord.
Leviticus6:23 No part of a grain offering that a priest makes may be eaten; all of it must be burned.
Leviticus6:24 The Lord commanded Moses
Leviticus6:25 to give Aaron and his sons the following regulations for sin offerings. The animal for a sin offering shall be killed on the north side of the altar
Leviticus6:26 The priest who sacrifices the animal shall eat it in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 Anyone or anything that touches the flesh of the animal will be harmed by the power of its holiness. If any article of clothing is spattered with the animal's blood
Leviticus6:28 Any clay pot in which the meat is boiled must be broken
Leviticus6:29 Any male of the priestly families may eat this offering; it is very holy.
Leviticus6:30 But if any of the blood is brought into the Tent and used in the ritual to take away sin
Leviticus7:1 The following are the regulations for repayment offerings
Leviticus7:2 The animal for this offering is to be killed on the north side of the altar
Leviticus7:3 All of its fat shall be removed and offered on the altar: the fat tail
Leviticus7:4 the kidneys and the fat on them
Leviticus7:5 The priest shall burn all the fat on the altar as a food offering to the Lord. It is a repayment offering.
Leviticus7:6 Any male of the priestly families may eat it
Leviticus7:7 There is one regulation that applies to both the sin offering and the repayment offering: the meat belongs to the priest who offers the sacrifice.
Leviticus7:8 The skin of an animal offered as a burnt offering belongs to the priest who offers the sacrifice.
Leviticus7:9 Every grain offering that has been baked in an oven or prepared in a pan or on a griddle belongs to the priest who has offered it to God.
Leviticus7:10 But all uncooked grain offerings
Leviticus7:11 The following are the regulations for the fellowship offerings presented to the Lord.
Leviticus7:12 If you make this offering as a thanksgiving offering to God
Leviticus7:13 In addition
Leviticus7:14 You shall present one part of each kind of bread as a special contribution to the Lord; it belongs to the priest who takes the blood of the animal and throws it against the altar.
Leviticus7:15 The flesh of the animal must be eaten on the day it is sacrificed; none of it may be left until the next morning.
Leviticus7:16 If you bring a fellowship offering as fulfillment of a vow or as your own freewill offering
Leviticus7:17 Any meat that still remains on the third day must be burned.
Leviticus7:18 If any of it is eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 If the meat comes into contact with anything ritually unclean
Leviticus7:20 but if any of you who are not clean eat it
Leviticus7:21 Also
Leviticus7:22 The Lord gave Moses the following regulations
Leviticus7:23 for the people of Israel. No fat of cattle
Leviticus7:24 The fat of an animal that has died a natural death or has been killed by a wild animal must not be eaten
Leviticus7:25 Anyone who eats the fat of an animal that may be offered as a food offering to the Lord will no longer be considered one of God's people.
Leviticus7:26 No matter where the Israelites live
Leviticus7:27 Anyone who breaks this law will no longer be considered one of God's people.
Leviticus7:28 The Lord gave Moses the following regulations
Leviticus7:29 for the people of Israel. When any of you offer a fellowship offering you must bring part of it as a special gift to the Lord
Leviticus7:30 bringing it with your own hands as a food offering. You shall bring the fat of the animal with its breast and present it as a special gift to the Lord.
Leviticus7:31 The priest shall burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus7:32 The right hind leg of the animal shall be given as a special contribution
Leviticus7:33 to the priest who offers the blood and the fat of the fellowship offering.
Leviticus7:34 The breast of the animal is a special gift
Leviticus7:35 This is the part of the food offered to the Lord that was given to Aaron and his sons on the day they were ordained as priests.
Leviticus7:36 On that day the Lord commanded the people of Israel to give them this part of the offering. It is a regulation that the people of Israel must obey for all time to come.
Leviticus7:37 These
Leviticus7:38 There on Mount Sinai in the desert
Leviticus8:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus8:2 “Take Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent of my presence and bring the priestly garments
Leviticus8:3 Then call the whole community together there.”
Leviticus8:4 Moses did as the Lord had commanded
Leviticus8:5 he said to them
Leviticus8:6 Moses brought Aaron and his sons forward and had them take a ritual bath.
Leviticus8:7 He put the shirt and the robe on Aaron and the sash around his waist. He put the ephod on him and fastened it by putting its finely woven belt around his waist.
Leviticus8:8 He put the breastpiece on him and put the Urim and Thummim in it.
Leviticus8:9 He placed the turban on his head
Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and put it on the Tent of the Lord 's presence and everything that was in it
Leviticus8:11 He took some of the oil and sprinkled it seven times on the altar and its equipment and on the basin and its base
Leviticus8:12 He ordained Aaron by pouring some of the anointing oil on his head.
Leviticus8:13 Next
Leviticus8:14 Then Moses brought the young bull for the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 Moses killed it and took some of the blood
Leviticus8:16 Moses took all the fat on the internal organs
Leviticus8:17 He took the rest of the bull
Leviticus8:18 Next
Leviticus8:19 Moses killed it and threw the blood on all four sides of the altar.
Leviticus8:20 He cut the ram in pieces
Leviticus8:21 
Leviticus8:22 Then Moses brought the second ram
Leviticus8:23 Moses killed it and took some of the blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron's right ear
Leviticus8:24 Then he brought Aaron's sons forward and put some of the blood on the lobes of their right ears
Leviticus8:25 He took the fat
Leviticus8:26 Then he took one loaf of bread from the basket of unleavened bread dedicated to the Lord
Leviticus8:27 He put all of this food in the hands of Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took the food from them and burned it on the altar
Leviticus8:29 Then Moses took the breast and presented it as a special gift to the Lord. It was Moses' part of the ordination ram. Moses did everything just as the Lord had commanded.
Leviticus8:30 Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled them on Aaron and his sons and on their clothes. In this way he consecrated them and their clothes to the Lord.
Leviticus8:31 Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 Burn up any meat or bread that is left over.
Leviticus8:33 You shall not leave the entrance of the Tent for seven days
Leviticus8:34 The Lord commanded us to do what we have done today
Leviticus8:35 You must stay at the entrance of the Tent day and night for seven days
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did everything that the Lord had commanded through Moses.
Leviticus9:1 The day after the ordination rites were completed
Leviticus9:2 He said to Aaron
Leviticus9:3 Then tell the people of Israel to take a male goat for a sin offering
Leviticus9:4 and a bull and a ram for a fellowship offering. They are to sacrifice them to the Lord with the grain offering mixed with oil. They must do this because the Lord will appear to them today.”
Leviticus9:5 They brought to the front of the Tent everything that Moses had commanded
Leviticus9:6 Moses said
Leviticus9:7 Then he said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 Aaron went to the altar and killed the young bull which was for his own sin offering.
Leviticus9:9 His sons brought him the blood
Leviticus9:10 Then he burned on the altar the fat
Leviticus9:11 But he burned the meat and the skin outside the camp.
Leviticus9:12 He killed the animal which was for his own burnt offering. His sons brought him the blood
Leviticus9:13 They handed him the head and the other pieces of the animal
Leviticus9:14 Then he washed the internal organs and the hind legs and burned them on the altar on top of the rest of the burnt offering.
Leviticus9:15 After that
Leviticus9:16 He also brought the animal for the burnt offering and offered it according to the regulations.
Leviticus9:17 He presented the grain offering and took a handful of flour and burned it on the altar. (This was in addition to the daily burnt offering.)
Leviticus9:18 He killed the bull and the ram as a fellowship offering for the people. His sons brought him the blood
Leviticus9:19 Aaron put the fat parts of the bull and the ram
Leviticus9:20 on top of the breasts of the animals and carried it all to the altar. He burned the fat on the altar
Leviticus9:21 and presented the breasts and the right hind legs as the special gift to the Lord for the priests
Leviticus9:22 When Aaron had finished all the sacrifices
Leviticus9:23 Moses and Aaron went into the Tent of the Lord 's presence
Leviticus9:24 Suddenly the Lord sent a fire
Leviticus10:1 Aaron's sons
Leviticus10:2 Suddenly the Lord sent fire
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus10:4 Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan
Leviticus10:5 So they came and took hold of the clothing on the corpses and carried them outside the camp
Leviticus10:6 Then Moses said to Aaron and to his sons Eleazar and Ithamar
Leviticus10:7 Do not leave the entrance of the Tent or you will die
Leviticus10:8 The Lord said to Aaron
Leviticus10:9 “You and your sons are not to enter the Tent of my presence after drinking wine or beer; if you do
Leviticus10:10 You must distinguish between what belongs to God and what is for general use
Leviticus10:11 You must teach the people of Israel all the laws which I have given to you through Moses.”
Leviticus10:12 Moses said to Aaron and his two remaining sons
Leviticus10:13 Eat it in a holy place; it is the part that belongs to you and your sons from the food offered to the Lord. That is what the Lord commanded me.
Leviticus10:14 But you and your families may eat the breast and the hind leg that are presented as the special gift and the special contribution to the Lord for the priests. You may eat them in any ritually clean place. These offerings have been given to you and your children as the part that belongs to you from the fellowship offerings of the people of Israel.
Leviticus10:15 They shall bring the hind leg and the breast at the time the fat is presented as a food offering to the Lord. These parts belong to you and your children forever

Leviticus13:6 The priest shall examine you again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 But if the sore spreads after the priest has examined you and pronounced you clean

Leviticus14:1 The Lord gave Moses
Leviticus14:2 the following regulations about the ritual purification of those of you cured of a dreaded skin disease. On the day you are to be pronounced clean
Leviticus14:3 and the priest shall take you outside the camp and examine you. If the disease is healed
Leviticus14:4 the priest shall order that two ritually clean birds be brought
Leviticus14:5 Then the priest shall order that one of the birds be killed over a clay bowl containing fresh spring water.
Leviticus14:6 He shall take the other bird and dip it
Leviticus14:7 He shall sprinkle the blood seven times on the one of you who is to be purified from your skin disease
Leviticus14:8 You shall wash your clothes
Leviticus14:9 On the seventh day you shall again shave your head
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day you shall bring two male lambs and one female lamb a year old that are without any defects
Leviticus14:11 The priest shall take you and these offerings to the entrance of the Tent of the Lord 's presence.
Leviticus14:12 Then the priest shall take one of the male lambs and together with the half pint of oil he shall offer it as a repayment offering. He shall present them as a special gift to the Lord for the priest.
Leviticus14:13 He shall kill the lamb in the holy place where the animals for the sin offerings and the burnt offerings are killed. He must do this because the repayment offering
Leviticus14:14 The priest shall take some of the blood of the lamb and put it on the lobe of the right ear
Leviticus14:15 The priest shall take some of the olive oil and pour it in the palm of his own left hand
Leviticus14:16 dip a finger of his right hand in it
Leviticus14:17 He shall take some of the oil that is in the palm of his hand and some of the blood of the lamb and put them on the lobe of the right ear
Leviticus14:18 He shall put the rest of the oil that is in the palm of his hand on your head. In this way he shall perform the ritual of purification.
Leviticus14:19 Then the priest shall offer the sin offering and perform the ritual of purification. After that
Leviticus14:20 and offer it with the grain offering on the altar. In this way the priest shall perform the ritual of purification
Leviticus14:21 If you are poor and cannot afford any more
Leviticus14:22 You shall also bring two doves or two pigeons
Leviticus14:23 On the eighth day of your purification you shall bring them to the priest at the entrance of the Tent.

Leviticus14:25 He shall kill the lamb and take some of the blood and put it on the lobe of your right ear
Leviticus14:26 The priest shall pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand
Leviticus14:27 and with a finger of his right hand sprinkle some of it seven times there in the Lord 's presence.
Leviticus14:28 He shall put some of the oil on the same places he put the blood: on the lobe of your right ear
Leviticus14:29 The rest of the oil that is in his palm he shall put on your head and in this way perform the ritual of purification.
Leviticus14:30 Then he shall offer one of the doves or pigeons
Leviticus14:31 as the sin offering and the other as the burnt offering with the grain offering. In this way the priest shall perform the ritual of purification.
Leviticus14:32 This is the law for those who have a dreaded skin disease but who cannot afford the normal offerings required for his purification.

Leviticus14:35

Leviticus14:37 and examine the mildew. If there are greenish or reddish spots that appear to be eating into the wall
Leviticus14:38 he shall leave the house and lock it up for seven days.
Leviticus14:39 On the seventh day he shall return and examine it again. If the mildew has spread
Leviticus14:40 he shall order that the stones on which the mildew is found be removed and thrown into some unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:41 After that he must have all the interior walls scraped and the plaster dumped in an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:42 Then other stones are to be used to replace the stones that were removed
Leviticus14:43 If the mildew breaks out again in the house after the stones have been removed and the house has been scraped and plastered
Leviticus14:44 the priest shall go and look. If it has spread
Leviticus14:45 It must be torn down
Leviticus14:46 Any who enter the house while it is locked up will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus14:47 Any who lie down or eat in the house must wash their clothes.

Leviticus14:49 To purify the house

Leviticus16:4 Then the Lord gave the following instructions. Before Aaron goes into the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:5 The community of Israel shall give Aaron two male goats for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 He shall offer a bull as a sacrifice to take away his own sins and those of his family.
Leviticus16:7 Then he shall take the two goats to the entrance of the Tent of the Lord 's presence.
Leviticus16:8 There he shall draw lots
Leviticus16:9 Aaron shall sacrifice the goat chosen by lot for the Lord and offer it as a sin offering.
Leviticus16:10 The goat chosen for Azazel shall be presented alive to the Lord and sent off into the desert to Azazel
Leviticus16:11 When Aaron sacrifices the bull as the sin offering for himself and his family
Leviticus16:12 he shall take a fire pan full of burning coals from the altar and two handfuls of fine incense and bring them into the Most Holy Place.
Leviticus16:13 There in the Lord 's presence he shall put the incense on the fire
Leviticus16:14 He shall take some of the bull's blood and with his finger sprinkle it on the front of the lid and then sprinkle some of it seven times in front of the Covenant Box.
Leviticus16:15 After that
Leviticus16:16 In this way he will perform the ritual to purify the Most Holy Place from the uncleanness of the people of Israel and from all their sins. He must do this to the Tent
Leviticus16:17 From the time Aaron enters the Most Holy Place to perform the ritual of purification until he comes out
Leviticus16:18 he must then go out to the altar for burnt offerings and purify it. He must take some of the bull's blood and some of the goat's blood and put it all over the projections at the corners of the altar.
Leviticus16:19 With his finger he must sprinkle some of the blood on the altar seven times. In this way he is to purify it from the sins of the people of Israel and make it holy.
Leviticus16:20 When Aaron has finished performing the ritual to purify the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:21 He shall put both of his hands on the goat's head and confess over it all the evils
Leviticus16:22 The goat will carry all their sins away with him into some uninhabited land.
Leviticus16:23 Then Aaron shall go into the Tent
Leviticus16:24 He must take a bath in a holy place and put on his own clothes. After that
Leviticus16:25 He shall burn on the altar the fat of the animal for the sin offering.
Leviticus16:26 The man who drove the goat into the desert to Azazel must wash his clothes and take a bath before he comes back into camp.
Leviticus16:27 The bull and the goat used for the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 The one who burns them must wash his clothes and take a bath before he returns to camp.
Leviticus16:29 The following regulations are to be observed for all time to come. On the tenth day of the seventh month the Israelites and the foreigners living among them must fast and must not do any work.
Leviticus16:30 On that day the ritual is to be performed to purify them from all their sins
Leviticus16:31 That day is to be a very holy day
Leviticus16:32 The High Priest
Leviticus16:33 and perform the ritual to purify the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:34 These regulations are to be observed for all time to come. This ritual must be performed once a year to purify the people of Israel from all their sins. So Moses did as the Lord had commanded.

Leviticus19:1 The Lord told Moses
Leviticus19:2 to say to the community of Israel
Leviticus19:3 Each of you must respect your mother and your father
Leviticus19:4 “Do not abandon me and worship idols; do not make gods of metal and worship them. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:5 “When you kill an animal for a fellowship offering
Leviticus19:6 The meat must be eaten on the day the animal is killed or on the next day. Any meat left on the third day must be burned
Leviticus19:7 because it is ritually unclean
Leviticus19:8 Any who eat it will be guilty of treating as ordinary what is dedicated to me
Leviticus19:9 “When you harvest your fields
Leviticus19:10 Do not go back through your vineyard to gather the grapes that were missed or to pick up the grapes that have fallen; leave them for poor people and foreigners. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:11 “Do not steal or cheat or lie.
Leviticus19:12 Do not make a promise in my name if you do not intend to keep it; that brings disgrace on my name. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:13 “Do not rob or take advantage of anyone. Do not hold back the wages of someone you have hired
Leviticus19:14 Do not curse the deaf or put something in front of the blind so as to make them stumble over it. Obey me; I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:15 “Be honest and just when you make decisions in legal cases; do not show favoritism to the poor or fear the rich.
Leviticus19:16 Do not spread lies about anyone
Leviticus19:17 “Do not bear a grudge against others
Leviticus19:18 Do not take revenge on others or continue to hate them
Leviticus19:19 “Obey my commands. Do not crossbreed domestic animals. Do not plant two kinds of seed in the same field. Do not wear clothes made of two kinds of material.
Leviticus19:20 “If a slave woman is the recognized concubine of a man and she has not been paid for and freed
Leviticus19:21 The man shall bring a ram to the entrance of the Tent of my presence as his repayment offering
Leviticus19:22 and with it the priest shall perform the ritual of purification to remove the man's sin
Leviticus19:23 “When you come into the land of Canaan and plant any kind of fruit tree
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year all the fruit shall be dedicated as an offering to show your gratitude to me
Leviticus19:25 But in the fifth year you may eat the fruit. If you do all this
Leviticus19:26 “Do not eat any meat with blood still in it. Do not practice any kind of magic.
Leviticus19:27 Do not cut the hair on the sides of your head or trim your beard
Leviticus19:28 or tattoo yourselves or cut gashes in your body to mourn for the dead. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:29 “Do not disgrace your daughters by making them temple prostitutes; if you do
Leviticus19:30 Keep the Sabbath
Leviticus19:31 “Do not go for advice to people who consult the spirits of the dead. If you do
Leviticus19:32 “Show respect for old people and honor them. Reverently obey me; I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:33 “Do not mistreat foreigners who are living in your land.
Leviticus19:34 Treat them as you would an Israelite
Leviticus19:35 “Do not cheat anyone by using false measures of length
Leviticus19:36 Use honest scales
Leviticus19:37 Obey all my laws and commands. I am the Lord.”

Leviticus21:4 He shall not make himself unclean at the death of those related to him by marriage.
Leviticus21:5 “No priest shall shave any part of his head or trim his beard or cut gashes on his body to show that he is in mourning.
Leviticus21:6 He must be holy and must not disgrace my name. He offers food offerings to me
Leviticus21:7 A priest shall not marry a woman who has been a prostitute or a woman who is not a virgin or who is divorced; he is holy.
Leviticus21:8 The people must consider the priest holy
Leviticus21:9 If a priest's daughter becomes a prostitute
Leviticus21:10 “The High Priest has had the anointing oil poured on his head and has been consecrated to wear the priestly garments
Leviticus21:11 He has been dedicated to me and is not to make himself ritually unclean nor is he to defile my sacred Tent by leaving it and entering a house where there is a dead person
Leviticus21:12 
Leviticus21:13 He shall marry a virgin
Leviticus21:14 not a widow or a divorced woman or a woman who has been a prostitute. He shall marry only a virgin from his own clan.
Leviticus21:15 Otherwise
Leviticus21:16 The Lord commanded Moses
Leviticus21:17 to tell Aaron
Leviticus21:18 No man with any physical defects may make the offering: no one who is blind
Leviticus21:19 no one with a crippled hand or foot;
Leviticus21:20 no one who is a hunchback or a dwarf; no one with any eye or skin disease; and no eunuch.
Leviticus21:21 No descendant of Aaron the priest who has any physical defects may present the food offering to me.
Leviticus21:22 Such a man may eat the food offered to me
Leviticus21:23 but because he has a physical defect
Leviticus21:24 This
Leviticus22:1 The Lord commanded Moses
Leviticus22:2 to tell Aaron and his sons
Leviticus22:3 If any of your descendants
Leviticus22:4 “None of the descendants of Aaron who has a dreaded skin disease or a discharge may eat any of the sacred offerings until he is ritually clean. Any priest is unclean if he touches anything which is unclean through contact with a corpse or if he has an emission of semen
Leviticus22:5 or if he has touched an unclean animal or person.
Leviticus22:6 Any priest who becomes unclean remains unclean until evening
Leviticus22:7 After the sun sets he is clean
Leviticus22:8 He shall not eat the meat of any animal that has died a natural death or has been killed by wild animals; it will make him unclean. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:9 “All priests shall observe the regulations that I have given. Otherwise
Leviticus22:10 “Only a member of a priestly family may eat any of the sacred offerings; no one else may eat them—not even someone staying with a priest or hired by him.
Leviticus22:11 But a priest's slaves
Leviticus22:12 A priest's daughter who marries someone who is not a priest may not eat any of the sacred offerings.
Leviticus22:13 But a widowed or divorced daughter who has no children and who has returned to live in her father's house as a dependent may eat the food her father receives as a priest. Only a member of a priestly family may eat any of it.
Leviticus22:14 “If any people who are not members of a priestly family eat any of the sacred offerings without intending to
Leviticus22:15 The priests shall not profane the sacred offerings
Leviticus22:16 by letting any unauthorized people eat them; this would bring guilt and punishment on such people. I am the Lord and I make the offerings holy.”
Leviticus22:17 The Lord commanded Moses
Leviticus22:18 to give Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel the following regulations. When any Israelite or any foreigner living in Israel presents a burnt offering
Leviticus22:19 To be accepted
Leviticus22:20 If you offer any animal that has any defects
Leviticus22:21 When anyone presents a fellowship offering to the Lord
Leviticus22:22 Do not offer to the Lord any animal that is blind or crippled or mutilated
Leviticus22:23 As a freewill offering you may offer an animal that is stunted or not perfectly formed
Leviticus22:24 Do not offer to the Lord any animal whose testicles have been crushed
Leviticus22:25 Do not offer as a food offering any animal obtained from a foreigner. Such animals are considered defective and are not acceptable.
Leviticus22:26 When a calf or a lamb or a kid is born
Leviticus22:27 
Leviticus22:28 Do not sacrifice a cow and its calf or a sheep and its lamb or a goat and its kid on the same day.
Leviticus22:29 When you offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the Lord
Leviticus22:30 eat it the same day and leave none of it until the next morning.
Leviticus22:31 The Lord said
Leviticus22:32 Do not bring disgrace on my holy name; all the people of Israel must acknowledge me to be holy. I am the Lord and I make you holy;
Leviticus22:33 and I brought you out of Egypt to become your God. I am the Lord.”
Leviticus23:1 The Lord gave Moses
Leviticus23:2 the following regulations for the religious festivals
Leviticus23:3 You have six days in which to do your work
Leviticus23:4 Proclaim the following festivals at the appointed times.
Leviticus23:5 The Passover
Leviticus23:6 On the fifteenth day the Festival of Unleavened Bread begins
Leviticus23:7 On the first of these days you shall gather for worship and do none of your daily work.
Leviticus23:8 Offer your food offerings to the Lord for seven days. On the seventh day you shall again gather for worship
Leviticus23:9 When you come into the land that the Lord is giving you and you harvest your grain
Leviticus23:10 
Leviticus23:11 He shall present it as a special offering to the Lord
Leviticus23:12 On the day you present the offering of grain
Leviticus23:13 With it you shall present four pounds of flour mixed with olive oil as a food offering. The odor of this offering is pleasing to the Lord. You shall also present with it an offering of one quart of wine.
Leviticus23:14 Do not eat any of the new grain
Leviticus23:15 Count seven full weeks from the day after the Sabbath on which you bring your sheaf of grain to present to the Lord.
Leviticus23:16 On the fiftieth day
Leviticus23:17 Each family is to bring two loaves of bread and present them to the Lord as a special gift. Each loaf shall be made of four pounds of flour baked with yeast and shall be presented to the Lord as an offering of the first grain to be harvested.
Leviticus23:18 And with the bread the community is to present seven one-year-old lambs
Leviticus23:19 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering and two one-year-old male lambs as a fellowship offering.
Leviticus23:20 The priest shall present the bread with the two lambs as a special gift to the Lord for the priests. These offerings are holy.
Leviticus23:21 On that day do none of your daily work
Leviticus23:22 When you harvest your fields
Leviticus23:23 On the first day of the seventh month observe a special day of rest
Leviticus23:24 
Leviticus23:25 Present a food offering to the Lord and do none of your daily work.
Leviticus23:26 The tenth day of the seventh month is the day when the annual ritual is to be performed to take away the sins of the people. On that day do not eat anything at all; come together for worship
Leviticus23:27 
Leviticus23:28 Do no work on that day
Leviticus23:29 Any who eat anything on that day will no longer be considered God's people.
Leviticus23:30 And if any do any work on that day
Leviticus23:31 This regulation applies to all your descendants
Leviticus23:32 From sunset on the ninth day of the month to sunset on the tenth observe this day as a special day of rest
Leviticus23:33 The Festival of Shelters begins on the fifteenth day of the seventh month and continues for seven days.
Leviticus23:34 
Leviticus23:35 On the first of these days come together for worship and do none of your daily work.
Leviticus23:36 Each day for seven days you shall present a food offering. On the eighth day come together again for worship and present a food offering. It is a day for worship
Leviticus23:37 These are the religious festivals on which you honor the Lord by gathering together for worship and presenting food offerings
Leviticus23:38 These festivals are in addition to the regular Sabbaths
Leviticus23:39 When you have harvested your fields
Leviticus23:40 On that day take some of the best fruit from your trees
Leviticus23:41 Celebrate it for seven days. This regulation is to be kept by your descendants for all time to come.
Leviticus23:42 All the people of Israel shall live in shelters for seven days
Leviticus23:43 so that your descendants may know that the Lord made the people of Israel live in simple shelters when he led them out of Egypt. He is the Lord your God.
Leviticus23:44 So in this way Moses gave the people of Israel the regulations for observing the religious festivals to honor the Lord.
Leviticus24:1 The Lord told Moses
Leviticus24:2 to give the following orders to the people of Israel: Bring pure olive oil of the finest quality for the lamps in the Tent
Leviticus24:3 Each evening Aaron shall light them and keep them burning until morning
Leviticus24:4 Aaron shall take care of the lamps on the lampstand of pure gold and must see that they burn regularly in the Lord 's presence.
Leviticus24:5 Take twenty-four pounds of flour and bake twelve loaves of bread.
Leviticus24:6 Put the loaves in two rows
Leviticus24:7 Put some pure incense on each row
Leviticus24:8 Every Sabbath
Leviticus24:9 The bread belongs to Aaron and his descendants
Leviticus24:10 There was a man whose father was an Egyptian and whose mother was an Israelite named Shelomith
Leviticus24:11 
Leviticus24:12 put him under guard
Leviticus24:13 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:14 “Take that man out of the camp. Everyone who heard him curse shall put his hands on the man's head to testify that he is guilty
Leviticus24:15 Then tell the people of Israel that anyone who curses God must suffer the consequences
Leviticus24:16 and be put to death. Any Israelite or any foreigner living in Israel who curses the Lord shall be stoned to death by the whole community.
Leviticus24:17 “Any who commit murder shall be put to death
Leviticus24:18 and any who kill an animal belonging to someone else must replace it. The principle is a life for a life.
Leviticus24:19 “If any of you injure another person
Leviticus24:20 If you break a bone
Leviticus24:21 Whoever kills an animal shall replace it
Leviticus24:22 This law applies to all of you
Leviticus24:23 When Moses had said this to the people of Israel
Leviticus25:1 The Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai and commanded him
Leviticus25:2 to give the following regulations to the people of Israel. When you enter the land that the Lord is giving you
Leviticus25:3 You shall plant your fields
Leviticus25:4 But the seventh year is to be a year of complete rest for the land
Leviticus25:5 Do not even harvest the grain that grows by itself without being planted
Leviticus25:6 Although the land has not been cultivated during that year
Leviticus25:7 your domestic animals
Leviticus25:8 Count seven times seven years
Leviticus25:9 Then
Leviticus25:10 In this way you shall set the fiftieth year apart and proclaim freedom to all the inhabitants of the land. During this year all property that has been sold shall be restored to the original owner or the descendants
Leviticus25:11 You shall not plant your fields or harvest the grain that grows by itself or gather the grapes in your unpruned vineyards.
Leviticus25:12 The whole year shall be sacred for you; you shall eat only what the fields produce of themselves.
Leviticus25:13 In this year all property that has been sold shall be restored to its original owner.
Leviticus25:14 So when you sell land to an Israelite or buy land
Leviticus25:15 The price is to be set according to the number of years the land can produce crops before the next Year of Restoration.
Leviticus25:16 If there are many years
Leviticus25:17 Do not cheat an Israelite
Leviticus25:18 Obey all the Lord 's laws and commands
Leviticus25:19 The land will produce its crops
Leviticus25:20 But someone may ask what there will be to eat during the seventh year
Leviticus25:21 The Lord will bless the land in the sixth year so that it will produce enough food for two years.
Leviticus25:22 When you plant your fields in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 Your land must not be sold on a permanent basis
Leviticus25:24 When land is sold
Leviticus25:25 If any of you Israelites become poor and are forced to sell your land
Leviticus25:26 If you have no relative to buy it back
Leviticus25:27 In that case you must pay to the one who bought it a sum that will make up for the years remaining until the next Year of Restoration
Leviticus25:28 But if you do not have enough money to buy the land back
Leviticus25:29 If you sell a house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 But if you do not buy it back within the year
Leviticus25:31 But houses in unwalled villages are to be treated like fields; the original owner has the right to buy them back
Leviticus25:32 However
Leviticus25:33 If a house in one of these cities is sold by a Levite and is not bought back
Leviticus25:34 But the pasture land around the Levite cities shall never be sold; it is their property forever.
Leviticus25:35 If any Israelites living near you become poor and cannot support themselves
Leviticus25:36 Do not charge Israelites any interest
Leviticus25:37 Do not make them pay interest on the money you lend them
Leviticus25:38 This is the command of the Lord your God
Leviticus25:39 If any Israelites living near you become so poor that they sell themselves to you as a slave
Leviticus25:40 They shall stay with you as hired workers and serve you until the next Year of Restoration.
Leviticus25:41 At that time they and their children shall leave you and return to their family and to the property of their ancestors.
Leviticus25:42 The people of Israel are the Lord 's slaves
Leviticus25:43 Do not treat them harshly
Leviticus25:44 If you need slaves
Leviticus25:45 You may also buy the children of the foreigners who are living among you. Such children born in your land may become your property
Leviticus25:46 and you may leave them as an inheritance to your children
Leviticus25:47 Suppose a foreigner living with you becomes rich
Leviticus25:48 After they are sold
Leviticus25:49 or an uncle or a cousin or another close relative may buy them back; or if they themselves earn enough
Leviticus25:50 They must consult the one who bought them
Leviticus25:51 They must refund a part of the purchase price according to the number of years left
Leviticus25:52 
Leviticus25:53 as if they had been hired on an annual basis. Their master must not treat them harshly.
Leviticus25:54 If they are not set free in any of these ways
Leviticus25:55 Israelites cannot be permanent slaves
Leviticus26:1 The Lord said
Leviticus26:2 Keep the religious festivals and honor the place where I am worshiped. I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 “If you live according to my laws and obey my commands
Leviticus26:4 I will send you rain at the right time
Leviticus26:5 Your crops will be so plentiful that you will still be harvesting grain when it is time to pick grapes
Leviticus26:6 “I will give you peace in your land
Leviticus26:7 You will be victorious over your enemies;
Leviticus26:8 five of you will be able to defeat a hundred
Leviticus26:9 I will bless you and give you many children; I will keep my part of the covenant that I made with you.
Leviticus26:10 Your harvests will be so plentiful that they will last for a year
Leviticus26:11 I will live among you in my sacred Tent
Leviticus26:12 I will be with you; I will be your God
Leviticus26:13 I
Leviticus26:14 The Lord said
Leviticus26:15 If you refuse to obey my laws and commands and break the covenant I have made with you
Leviticus26:16 I will punish you. I will bring disaster on you—incurable diseases and fevers that will make you blind and cause your life to waste away. You will plant your crops
Leviticus26:17 I will turn against you
Leviticus26:18 “If even after all of this you still do not obey me
Leviticus26:19 I will break your stubborn pride; there will be no rain
Leviticus26:20 All your hard work will do you no good
Leviticus26:21 “If you still continue to resist me and refuse to obey me
Leviticus26:22 I will send dangerous animals among you
Leviticus26:23 “If after all of this punishment you still do not listen to me
Leviticus26:24 then I will turn on you and punish you seven times harder than before.
Leviticus26:25 I will bring war on you to punish you for breaking our covenant
Leviticus26:26 I will cut off your food supply
Leviticus26:27 “If after all of this you still continue to defy me and refuse to obey me
Leviticus26:28 then in my anger I will turn on you and again make your punishment seven times worse than before.
Leviticus26:29 Your hunger will be so great that you will eat your own children.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your places of worship on the hills
Leviticus26:31 I will turn your cities into ruins
Leviticus26:32 I will destroy your land so completely that the enemies who occupy it will be shocked at the destruction.
Leviticus26:33 I will bring war on you and scatter you in foreign lands. Your land will be deserted
Leviticus26:34 Then the land will enjoy the years of complete rest that you would not give it; it will lie abandoned and get its rest while you are in exile in the land of your enemies.
Leviticus26:35 
Leviticus26:36 “I will make those of you who are in exile so terrified that the sound of a leaf blowing in the wind will make you run. You will run as if you were being pursued in battle
Leviticus26:37 You will stumble over one another when no one is chasing you
Leviticus26:38 You will die in exile
Leviticus26:39 The few of you who survive in the land of your enemies will waste away because of your own sin and the sin of your ancestors.
Leviticus26:40 “But your descendants will confess their sins and the sins of their ancestors
Leviticus26:41 and caused me to turn against them and send them into exile in the land of their enemies. At last
Leviticus26:42 I will remember my covenant with Jacob and with Isaac and with Abraham
Leviticus26:43 First
Leviticus26:44 But even then
Leviticus26:45 I will renew the covenant that I made with their ancestors when I showed all the nations my power by bringing my people out of Egypt
Leviticus26:46 All these are the laws and commands that the Lord gave to Moses on Mount Sinai for the people of Israel.

Leviticus27:29 Not even human beings who have been unconditionally dedicated may be bought back; they must be put to death.

Leviticus27:31 If you wish to buy any of it back

Leviticus27:33 You may not arrange the animals so that the poor animals are chosen

Numbers4:1 The Lord told Moses
Numbers4:2 to take a census of the Levite clan of Kohath by subclans and families
Numbers4:3 and to register all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who were qualified to work in the Tent of the Lord 's presence.
Numbers4:4 Their service involves the most holy things.
Numbers4:5 The Lord gave Moses the following instructions. When it is time to break camp
Numbers4:6 They shall put a fine leather cover over it
Numbers4:7 They shall spread a blue cloth over the table for the bread offered to the Lord and put on it the dishes
Numbers4:8 They shall spread a red cloth over all of this
Numbers4:9 They shall take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand
Numbers4:10 They shall wrap it and all its equipment in a fine leather cover and place it on a carrying frame.
Numbers4:11 Next they shall spread a blue cloth over the gold altar
Numbers4:12 They shall take all the utensils used in the Holy Place
Numbers4:13 They shall remove the greasy ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth over it.
Numbers4:14 They shall put on it all the equipment used in the service at the altar: fire pans
Numbers4:15 When it is time to break camp
Numbers4:16 Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall be responsible for the whole Tent and for the oil for the lamps
Numbers4:17 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 “Do not let the clan of Kohath
Numbers4:19 be killed by coming near these most sacred objects. To prevent this from happening
Numbers4:20 But if the Kohathites enter the Tent and see the priests preparing the sacred objects for moving

Numbers4:22 to take a census of the Levite clan of Gershon by subclans and families
Numbers4:23 and to register all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who were qualified to work in the Tent of the Lord 's presence.
Numbers4:24 They shall be responsible for carrying the following objects:
Numbers4:25 the Tent
Numbers4:26 the curtains and ropes for the court that is around the Tent and the altar
Numbers4:27 Moses and Aaron shall see to it that the Gershonites perform all the duties and carry everything that Aaron and his sons assign to them.
Numbers4:28 These are the responsibilities of the Gershon clan in the Tent; they shall carry them out under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.

Numbers4:30 and to register all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who were qualified to work in the Tent of the Lord 's presence.
Numbers4:31 They shall be responsible for carrying the frames
Numbers4:32 and the posts

Numbers5:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:2 “Command the people of Israel to expel from the camp everyone with a dreaded skin disease or a bodily discharge and everyone who is unclean by contact with a corpse.
Numbers5:3 Send all these ritually unclean people out
Numbers5:4 The Israelites obeyed and expelled them all from the camp.
Numbers5:5 The Lord gave Moses
Numbers5:6 the following instructions for the people of Israel. When any of you are unfaithful to the Lord and commit a wrong against someone
Numbers5:7 you must confess your sin and make full repayment
Numbers5:8 But if that person has died and has no near relative to whom payment can be made
Numbers5:9 Also every special contribution which the Israelites offer to the Lord belongs to the priest to whom they present it.
Numbers5:10 Each priest shall keep the offerings presented to him.
Numbers5:11 The Lord commanded Moses
Numbers5:12 to give the Israelites the following instructions. It may happen that a man becomes suspicious that his wife is unfaithful to him and has defiled herself by having intercourse with another man. But the husband may not be certain
Numbers5:13 
Numbers5:14 
Numbers5:15 In either case the man shall take his wife to the priest. He shall also take the required offering of two pounds of barley flour
Numbers5:16 The priest shall bring the woman forward and have her stand in front of the altar.
Numbers5:17 He shall pour some holy water into a clay bowl and take some of the earth that is on the floor of the Tent of the Lord 's presence and put it in the water to make it bitter.
Numbers5:18 Then he shall loosen the woman's hair and put the offering of flour in her hands. In his hands the priest shall hold the bowl containing the bitter water that brings a curse.
Numbers5:19 Then the priest shall make the woman agree to this oath spoken by the priest: “If you have not committed adultery
Numbers5:20 But if you have committed adultery
Numbers5:21 may the Lord make your name a curse among your people. May he cause your genital organs to shrink and your stomach to swell up.
Numbers5:22 May this water enter your stomach and cause it to swell up and your genital organs to shrink.” The woman shall respond
Numbers5:23 Then the priest shall write this curse down and wash the writing off into the bowl of bitter water.
Numbers5:24 Before he makes the woman drink the water
Numbers5:25 the priest shall take the offering of flour out of the woman's hands
Numbers5:26 Then he shall take a handful of it as a token offering and burn it on the altar. Finally
Numbers5:27 If she has committed adultery
Numbers5:28 But if she is innocent
Numbers5:29 This is the law in cases where a man is jealous and becomes suspicious that his wife has committed adultery. The woman shall be made to stand in front of the altar
Numbers5:30 
Numbers5:31 The husband shall be free of guilt

Numbers6:2 to give the following instructions to the people of Israel. Any of you

Numbers6:5 As long as you are under the nazirite vow
Numbers6:6 Your hair is the sign of your dedication to God
Numbers6:7 
Numbers6:8 As long as you are a nazirite
Numbers6:9 If your consecrated hair is defiled because you are right beside someone who suddenly dies

Numbers6:11 The priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering
Numbers6:12 and rededicate to the Lord your time as a nazirite. The previous period of time doesn't count
Numbers6:13 When you complete your nazirite vow
Numbers6:14 and present to the Lord three animals without any defects: a one-year-old male lamb for a burnt offering
Numbers6:15 You shall also offer a basket of bread made without yeast: thick loaves made of flour mixed with olive oil and thin cakes brushed with olive oil
Numbers6:16 The priest shall present all these to the Lord and offer the sin offering and the burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 He shall sacrifice the ram to the Lord as a fellowship offering
Numbers6:18 At the entrance of the Tent you nazirites shall shave off your hair and put it on the fire on which the fellowship offering is being burned.

Numbers18:31 You and your families may eat the rest anywhere

Numbers19:1 The Lord commanded Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 to give the Israelites the following regulations. Bring to Moses and Aaron a red cow which has no defects and which has never been worked
Numbers19:3 and they will give it to Eleazar the priest. It is to be taken outside the camp and killed in his presence.
Numbers19:4 Then Eleazar is to take some of its blood and with his finger sprinkle it seven times in the direction of the Tent.
Numbers19:5 The whole animal
Numbers19:6 Then he is to take some cedar wood
Numbers19:7 After that
Numbers19:8 The one who burned the cow must also wash his clothes and pour water over himself
Numbers19:9 Then someone who is ritually clean is to collect the ashes of the cow and put them in a ritually clean place outside the camp
Numbers19:10 The one who collected the ashes must wash his clothes
Numbers19:11 Those who touch a corpse are ritually unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 They must purify themselves with the water for purification on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 Those who touch a corpse and do not purify themselves remain unclean
Numbers19:14 In the case of a person who dies in a tent
Numbers19:15 Every jar and pot in the tent that has no lid on it also becomes unclean.
Numbers19:16 If any touch a person who has been killed or has died a natural death outdoors or if any touch a human bone or a grave
Numbers19:17 To remove the uncleanness
Numbers19:18 In the first case
Numbers19:19 On the third day and on the seventh the person who is ritually clean is to sprinkle the water on the unclean persons. On the seventh day he is to purify those
Numbers19:20 Those who have become ritually unclean and do not purify themselves remain unclean
Numbers19:21 You are to observe this rule for all time to come. The person who sprinkles the water for purification must also wash his clothes; anyone who touches the water remains ritually unclean until evening.
Numbers19:22 Whatever an unclean person touches is unclean
