Numbers28:1 The Lord commanded Moses
Numbers28:2 to instruct the Israelites to present to God at the appointed times the required food offerings that are pleasing to him.
Numbers28:3 These are the food offerings that are to be presented to the Lord: for the daily burnt offering
Numbers28:4 Offer the first lamb in the morning
Numbers28:5 each with a grain offering of 2 pounds of flour
Numbers28:6 This is the daily offering that is completely burned
Numbers28:7 As the wine offering with the first lamb
Numbers28:8 In the evening offer the second lamb in the same way as the morning offering
Numbers28:9 On the Sabbath day offer two one-year-old male lambs without any defects
Numbers28:10 This burnt offering is to be offered every Sabbath in addition to the daily offering with its wine offering.
Numbers28:11 Present a burnt offering to the Lord at the beginning of each month: two young bulls
Numbers28:12 As a grain offering
Numbers28:13 and with each lamb
Numbers28:14 The proper wine offering is 4 pints of wine with each bull
Numbers28:15 And in addition to the daily burnt offering with its wine offering
Numbers28:16 The Passover Festival in honor of the Lord is to be held on the fourteenth day of the first month.
Numbers28:17 On the fifteenth day a religious festival begins which lasts seven days
Numbers28:18 On the first day of the festival you are to gather for worship
Numbers28:19 Offer a burnt offering as a food offering to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:20 Offer the proper grain offering of flour mixed with olive oil: 6 pounds with each bull
Numbers28:21 and 2 pounds with each lamb.
Numbers28:22 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering
Numbers28:23 Offer these in addition to the regular morning burnt offering.
Numbers28:24 In the same way
Numbers28:25 Meet for worship on the seventh day and do no work.
Numbers28:26 On the first day of the Harvest Festival
Numbers28:27 Offer a burnt offering as an odor pleasing to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:28 Offer the proper grain offering of flour mixed with olive oil: 6 pounds with each bull
Numbers28:29 and 2 pounds with each lamb.
Numbers28:30 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering
Numbers28:31 Offer these and the wine offering in addition to the daily burnt offering and grain offering.
Numbers29:1 On the first day of the seventh month you are to gather for worship
Numbers29:2 Present a burnt offering to the Lord
Numbers29:3 Offer the proper grain offering of flour mixed with olive oil: 6 pounds of flour with the bull
Numbers29:4 and 2 pounds with each lamb.
Numbers29:5 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering
Numbers29:6 Offer these in addition to the regular burnt offering for the first day of the month with its grain offering
Numbers29:7 Gather for worship on the tenth day of the seventh month; eat no food and do no work.
Numbers29:8 Offer a burnt offering to the Lord
Numbers29:9 Offer the proper grain offering of flour mixed with olive oil: 6 pounds of flour with the bull
Numbers29:10 and 2 pounds with each lamb.
Numbers29:11 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering

Numbers29:15 and 2 pounds with each lamb
Numbers29:16 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. Offer these in addition to the daily burnt offering with its grain offering and wine offering.

Numbers29:22 
Numbers29:23 On the fourth day offer ten young bulls

Numbers29:25

Numbers29:27 Offer with them all the other offerings required for the first day.
Numbers29:28

Numbers29:31

Numbers29:34

Numbers30:1 Moses gave the following instructions to the leaders of the tribes of Israel.
Numbers30:2 When a man makes a vow to give something to the Lord or takes an oath to abstain from something
Numbers30:3 When a young woman still living in her father's house makes a vow to give something to the Lord or promises to abstain from something
Numbers30:4 she must do everything that she vowed or promised unless her father raises an objection when he hears about it.
Numbers30:5 But if her father forbids her to fulfill the vow when he hears about it
Numbers30:6 If an unmarried woman makes a vow
Numbers30:7 she must do everything that she vowed or promised unless her husband raises an objection when he hears about it.
Numbers30:8 But if her husband forbids her to fulfill the vow when he hears about it
Numbers30:9 A widow or a divorced woman must keep every vow she makes and every promise to abstain from something.
Numbers30:10 If a married woman makes a vow or promises to abstain from something
Numbers30:11 she must do everything that she vowed or promised unless her husband raises an objection when he hears about it.
Numbers30:12 But if her husband forbids her to fulfill the vow when he hears about it
Numbers30:13 Her husband has the right to affirm or to annul any vow or promise that she has made.
Numbers30:14 But if
Numbers30:15 But if he later annuls the vow
Numbers30:16 These are the rules that the Lord gave Moses concerning vows made by an unmarried woman living in her father's house or by a married woman.

Numbers35:1 In the plains of Moab across the Jordan from Jericho the Lord said to Moses

Numbers35:7 with their pasture land
Numbers35:8 The number of Levite cities in each tribe is to be determined according to the size of its territory.”
Numbers35:9 The Lord told Moses
Numbers35:10 to say to the people of Israel: “When you cross the Jordan River and enter the land of Canaan
Numbers35:11 you are to choose cities of refuge to which any of you can escape if you kill someone accidentally.
Numbers35:12 There you will be safe from the dead person's relative who seeks revenge. No one accused of manslaughter is to be put to death without a public trial.
Numbers35:13 Choose six cities
Numbers35:14 three east of the Jordan and three in the land of Canaan.
Numbers35:15 These will serve as cities of refuge for Israelites and for foreigners who are temporary or permanent residents. Anyone who kills someone accidentally can escape to one of them.
Numbers35:16 “If
Numbers35:17 
Numbers35:18 
Numbers35:19 The dead person's nearest relative has the responsibility for putting the murderer to death. When he finds you
Numbers35:20 “If you hate someone and kill him by pushing him down or by throwing something at him
Numbers35:21 or by striking him with your fist
Numbers35:22 “But suppose you accidentally kill someone you do not hate
Numbers35:23 Or suppose that
Numbers35:24 In such cases the community shall judge in your favor and not in favor of the dead person's relative who is seeking revenge.
Numbers35:25 You are guilty only of manslaughter
Numbers35:26 If you leave the city of refuge to which you have escaped
Numbers35:27 and if the dead person's relative finds you and kills you
Numbers35:28 Any of you guilty of manslaughter must remain in the city of refuge until the death of the High Priest
Numbers35:29 These rules apply to you and your descendants wherever you may live.
Numbers35:30 “Those accused of murder may be found guilty and put to death only on the evidence of two or more witnesses; the evidence of one witness is not sufficient to support an accusation of murder.
Numbers35:31 Murderers must be put to death. They cannot escape this penalty by the payment of money.
Numbers35:32 If they have fled to a city of refuge
Numbers35:33 If you did this
Numbers35:34 Do not defile the land where you are living

Joshua7:2 Joshua sent some men from Jericho to Ai
Joshua7:3 they reported back to Joshua: “There is no need for everyone to attack Ai. Send only about two or three thousand men. Don't send the whole army up there to fight; it is not a large city.”
Joshua7:4 So about three thousand Israelites made the attack
Joshua7:5 The men of Ai chased them from the city gate as far as some quarries and killed about thirty-six of them on the way down the hill. Then the Israelites lost their courage and were afraid.
Joshua7:6 Joshua and the leaders of Israel tore their clothes in grief
Joshua7:7 And Joshua said

Joshua8:2 You are to do to Ai and its king what you did to Jericho and its king

Joshua8:4 with these orders: “Hide on the other side of the city

Joshua8:6 They will pursue us until we have led them away from the city. They will think that we are running from them
Joshua8:7 Then you will come out of hiding and capture the city. The Lord your God will give it to you.
Joshua8:8 After you have taken the city
Joshua8:9 So Joshua sent them out
Joshua8:10 Early in the morning Joshua got up and called the soldiers together. Then he and the leaders of Israel led them to Ai.
Joshua8:11 The soldiers with him went toward the main entrance to the city and set up camp on the north side
Joshua8:12 He took about five thousand men and put them in hiding west of the city
Joshua8:13 The soldiers were arranged for battle with the main camp north of the city and the rest of the men to the west. Joshua spent the night in the valley.

Joshua8:23 except the king of Ai. He was captured and taken to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 The Israelites killed every one of the enemy in the barren country where they had chased them. Then they went back to Ai and killed everyone there.
Joshua8:25 Joshua kept his spear pointed at Ai and did not put it down until every person there had been killed. The whole population of Ai was killed that day—twelve thousand men and women.
Joshua8:26 
Joshua8:27 The Israelites kept for themselves the livestock and goods captured in the city

Joshua18:1 After they had conquered the land
Joshua18:2 There were still seven tribes of the people of Israel who had not yet been assigned their share of the land.
Joshua18:3 So Joshua said to the people of Israel
Joshua18:4 Let me have three men from each tribe. I will send them out over the whole country to map out the territory that they would like to have as their possession. Then they are to come back to me.
Joshua18:5 The land will be divided among them in seven parts; Judah will stay in its territory in the south
Joshua18:6 Write down a description of these seven divisions and bring it to me. Then I will draw lots to consult the Lord our God for you.
Joshua18:7 The Levites
Joshua18:8 The men went on their way to map out the land after Joshua had given them these instructions: “Go all over the land and map it out
Joshua18:9 So the men went all over the land and set down in writing how they divided it into seven parts
Joshua18:10 Joshua drew lots to consult the Lord for them
Joshua18:11 The territory belonging to the families of the tribe of Benjamin was the first to be assigned. Their land lay between the tribes of Judah and Joseph.
Joshua18:12 On the north their border began at the Jordan and then went up the slope north of Jericho and westward through the hill country as far as the desert of Bethaven.
Joshua18:13 The border then went to the slope on the south side of Luz (also called Bethel)
Joshua18:14 The border then went in another direction
Joshua18:15 The southern border started on the edge of Kiriath Jearim and went to the Springs of Nephtoah.
Joshua18:16 It then went down to the foot of the mountain that overlooks Hinnom Valley
Joshua18:17 It then turned north to Enshemesh and then on to Geliloth
Joshua18:18 and passed north of the ridge overlooking the Jordan Valley. It then went down into the valley
Joshua18:19 passing north of the ridge of Beth Hoglah
Joshua18:20 The Jordan was the eastern border. These were the borders of the land which the families of the tribe of Benjamin received as their possession.

Joshua22:8

Joshua22:16 and speaking for the whole community of the Lord

Joshua22:18 Are you going to refuse to follow him now? If you rebel against the Lord today
Joshua22:19 Now then
Joshua22:20 Remember how Achan son of Zerah refused to obey the command about the things condemned to destruction; the whole community of Israel was punished for that. Achan was not the only one who died because of his sin.”
Joshua22:21 The people of the tribes of Reuben
Joshua22:22 “The Mighty One is God! He is the Lord! The Mighty One is God! He is the Lord! He knows why we did this
Joshua22:23 If we disobeyed the Lord and built our own altar to burn sacrifices on or to use for grain offerings or fellowship offerings
Joshua22:24 No! We did it because we were afraid that in the future your descendants would say to ours
Joshua22:25 He made the Jordan a boundary between us and you people of Reuben and Gad. You have nothing to do with the Lord.’ Then your descendants might make our descendants stop worshiping the Lord.
Joshua22:26 So we built an altar
Joshua22:27 but instead
Joshua22:28 It was our idea that
Joshua22:29 We would certainly not rebel against the Lord or stop following him now by building an altar to burn offerings on or for grain offerings or sacrifices. We would not build any other altar than the altar of the Lord our God that stands in front of the Tent of his presence.”

Joshua22:31 Phinehas

Joshua23:6 So be careful to obey and do everything that is written in the book of the Law of Moses. Do not neglect any part of it
Joshua23:7 and then you will not associate with these peoples left among you or speak the names of their gods or use those names in taking vows or worship those gods or bow down to them.
Joshua23:8 Instead

Joshua23:10 Any one of you can make a thousand men run away
Joshua23:11 Be careful

Judges2:1 The angel of the Lord went from Gilgal to Bochim and said to the Israelites
Judges2:2 You must not make any covenant with the people who live in this land. You must tear down their altars.’ But you have not done what I told you. You have done just the opposite!
Judges2:3 So I tell you now that I will not drive these people out as you advance. They will be your enemies
Judges2:4 When the angel had said this
Judges2:5 and that is why the place is called Bochim. There they offered sacrifices to the Lord.
Judges2:6 Joshua sent the people of Israel on their way
Judges2:7 As long as Joshua lived
Judges2:8 The Lord 's servant Joshua son of Nun died at the age of a hundred and ten.
Judges2:9 He was buried in his own part of the land at Timnath Serah in the hill country of Ephraim north of Mount Gaash.
Judges2:10 That whole generation also died
Judges2:11 Then the people of Israel sinned against the Lord and began to serve the Baals.
Judges2:12 They stopped worshiping the Lord
Judges2:13 They stopped worshiping the Lord and served the Baals and the Astartes.
Judges2:14 And so the Lord became furious with Israel and let raiders attack and rob them. He let the enemies all around overpower them
Judges2:15 Every time they would go into battle
Judges2:16 Then the Lord gave the Israelites leaders who saved them from the raiders.
Judges2:17 But the Israelites paid no attention to their leaders. Israel was unfaithful to the Lord and worshiped other gods. Their fathers had obeyed the Lord 's commands
Judges2:18 Whenever the Lord gave Israel a leader
Judges2:19 But when the leader died
Judges2:20 Then the Lord would become furious with Israel and say
Judges2:21 I will no longer drive out any of the nations that were still in the land when Joshua died.
Judges2:22 I will use them to find out whether or not these Israelites will follow my ways
Judges2:23 So the Lord allowed these nations to remain in the land; he did not give Joshua victory over them
Judges3:1 So then
Judges3:2 He did this only in order to teach each generation of Israelites about war
Judges3:3 Those left in the land were the five Philistine cities
Judges3:4 They were to be a test for Israel
Judges3:5 And so the people of Israel settled down among the Canaanites
Judges3:6 They intermarried with them and worshiped their gods.
Judges3:7 The people of Israel forgot the Lord their God; they sinned against him and worshiped the idols of Baal and Asherah.
Judges3:8 So the Lord became angry with Israel and let King Cushan Rishathaim of Mesopotamia conquer them. They were subject to him for eight years.
Judges3:9 Then the Israelites cried out to the Lord
Judges3:10 The spirit of the Lord came upon him
Judges3:11 There was peace in the land for forty years
Judges3:12 The people of Israel sinned against the Lord again. Because of this the Lord made King Eglon of Moab stronger than Israel.
Judges3:13 Eglon joined the Ammonites and the Amalekites; they defeated Israel and captured Jericho
Judges3:14 The Israelites were subject to Eglon for eighteen years.

Judges3:25 They waited as long as they thought they should

Judges3:28 He told them

Judges6:1 Once again the people of Israel sinned against the Lord
Judges6:2 The Midianites were stronger than Israel
Judges6:3 Whenever the Israelites would plant their crops
Judges6:4 They would camp on the land and destroy the crops as far south as the area around Gaza. They would take all the sheep
Judges6:5 They would come with their livestock and tents
Judges6:6 and Israel was helpless against them.
Judges6:7 Then the people of Israel cried out to the Lord for help against the Midianites
Judges6:8 and he sent them a prophet who brought them this message from the Lord
Judges6:9 I rescued you from the Egyptians and from the people who fought you here in this land. I drove them out as you advanced
Judges6:10 I told you that I am the Lord your God and that you should not worship the gods of the Amorites
Judges6:11 Then the Lord 's angel came to the village of Ophrah and sat under the oak tree that belonged to Joash
Judges6:12 The Lord 's angel appeared to him there and said
Judges6:13 Gideon said to him
Judges6:14 Then the Lord ordered him
Judges6:15 Gideon replied
Judges6:16 The Lord answered
Judges6:17 Gideon replied
Judges6:18 Please do not leave until I bring you an offering of food.” He said
Judges6:19 So Gideon went into his house and cooked a young goat and used a bushel of flour to make bread without any yeast. He put the meat in a basket and the broth in a pot
Judges6:20 The angel told him
Judges6:21 Then the Lord 's angel reached out and touched the meat and the bread with the end of the stick he was holding. Fire came out of the rock and burned up the meat and the bread. Then the angel disappeared.
Judges6:22 Gideon then realized that it was the Lord 's angel he had seen
Judges6:23 But the Lord told him
Judges6:24 Gideon built an altar to the Lord there and named it “The Lord is Peace.” (It is still standing at Ophrah
Judges6:25 That night the Lord told Gideon
Judges6:26 Build a well-constructed altar to the Lord your God on top of this mound. Then take the second bull and burn it whole as an offering
Judges6:27 So Gideon took ten of his servants and did what the Lord had told him. He was too afraid of his family and the people in town to do it by day
Judges6:28 When the people in town got up early the next morning

1Kings3:3 Solomon loved the Lord and followed the instructions of his father David

1Kings3:13 I will also give you what you have not asked for: all your life you will have wealth and honor
1Kings3:14 And if you obey me and keep my laws and commands
1Kings3:15 Solomon woke up and realized that God had spoken to him in the dream. Then he went to Jerusalem and stood in front of the Lord 's Covenant Box and offered burnt offerings and fellowship offerings to the Lord. After that he gave a feast for all his officials.

1Kings4:1 Solomon was king of all Israel
1Kings4:2 and these were his high officials: The priest: Azariah son of Zadok
1Kings4:3 The court secretaries: Elihoreph and Ahijah
1Kings4:4 Commander of the army: Benaiah son of Jehoiada Priests: Zadok and Abiathar
1Kings4:5 Chief of the district governors: Azariah son of Nathan Royal Adviser: the priest Zabud son of Nathan
1Kings4:6 In charge of the palace servants: Ahishar In charge of the forced labor: Adoniram son of Abda
1Kings4:7 Solomon appointed twelve men as district governors in Israel. They were to provide food from their districts for the king and his household
1Kings4:8 The following are the names of these twelve officers and the districts they were in charge of: Benhur: the hill country of Ephraim
1Kings4:9 Bendeker: the cities of Makaz
1Kings4:10 Benhesed: the cities of Arubboth and Socoh and all the territory of Hepher
1Kings4:11 Benabinadab
1Kings4:12 Baana son of Ahilud: the cities of Taanach
1Kings4:13 Bengeber: the city of Ramoth in Gilead
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab son of Iddo: the district of Mahanaim
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz
1Kings4:16 Baana son of Hushai: the region of Asher and the town of Bealoth
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat son of Paruah: the territory of Issachar
1Kings4:18 Shimei son of Ela: the territory of Benjamin
1Kings4:19 Geber son of Uri: the region of Gilead
1Kings4:20 The people of Judah and Israel were as numerous as the grains of sand on the seashore; they ate and drank
1Kings4:21 Solomon's kingdom included all the nations from the Euphrates River to Philistia and the Egyptian border. They paid him taxes and were subject to him all his life.
1Kings4:22 The supplies Solomon needed each day were 150 bushels of fine flour and 300 bushels of meal;
1Kings4:23 10 stall-fed cattle
1Kings4:24 Solomon ruled over all the land west of the Euphrates River
1Kings4:25 As long as he lived
1Kings4:26 Solomon had forty thousand stalls for his chariot horses and twelve thousand cavalry horses.
1Kings4:27 His twelve governors
1Kings4:28 Each governor also supplied his share of barley and straw
1Kings4:29 God gave Solomon unusual wisdom and insight
1Kings4:30 Solomon was wiser than the wise men of the East or the wise men of Egypt.
1Kings4:31 He was the wisest of all men: wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite
1Kings4:32 He composed three thousand proverbs and more than a thousand songs.
1Kings4:33 He spoke of trees and plants
1Kings4:34 Kings all over the world heard of his wisdom and sent people to listen to him.

1Kings10:24 and the whole world wanted to come and listen to the wisdom that God had given him.
1Kings10:25 Everyone who came brought him a gift—articles of silver and gold
1Kings10:26 Solomon built up a force of fourteen hundred chariots and twelve thousand cavalry horses. Some of them he kept in Jerusalem and the rest he stationed in various other cities.
1Kings10:27 During his reign silver was as common in Jerusalem as stone
1Kings10:28 The king's agents controlled the export of horses from Musri and Cilicia
1Kings10:29 and the export of chariots from Egypt. They supplied the Hittite and Syrian kings with horses and chariots
1Kings11:1 Solomon loved many foreign women. Besides the daughter of the king of Egypt he married Hittite women and women from Moab
1Kings11:2 He married them even though the Lord had commanded the Israelites not to intermarry with these people
1Kings11:3 Solomon married seven hundred princesses and also had three hundred concubines. They made him turn away from God
1Kings11:4 and by the time he was old they had led him into the worship of foreign gods. He was not faithful to the Lord his God
1Kings11:5 He worshiped Astarte
1Kings11:6 He sinned against the Lord and was not true to him as his father David had been.

2Kings24:16 Nebuchadnezzar deported all the important men to Babylonia
2Kings24:17 Nebuchadnezzar made Jehoiachin's uncle Mattaniah king of Judah and changed his name to Zedekiah.
2Kings24:18 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king of Judah
2Kings24:19 King Zedekiah sinned against the Lord
2Kings24:20 The Lord became so angry with the people of Jerusalem and Judah that he banished them from his sight.
2Kings25:1 Zedekiah rebelled against King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylonia
2Kings25:2 and kept it under siege until Zedekiah's eleventh year.
2Kings25:3 On the ninth day of the fourth month of that same year
2Kings25:4 the city walls were broken through. Although the Babylonians were surrounding the city
2Kings25:5 But the Babylonian army pursued King Zedekiah
2Kings25:6 Zedekiah was taken to King Nebuchadnezzar
2Kings25:7 While Zedekiah was looking on
2Kings25:8 On the seventh day of the fifth month of the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylonia
2Kings25:9 He burned down the Temple
2Kings25:10 and his soldiers tore down the city walls.
2Kings25:11 Then Nebuzaradan took away to Babylonia the people who were left in the city
2Kings25:12 But he left in Judah some of the poorest people
2Kings25:13 The Babylonians broke in pieces the bronze columns and the carts that were in the Temple
2Kings25:14 They also took away the shovels and the ash containers used in cleaning the altar
2Kings25:15 They took away everything that was made of gold or silver
2Kings25:16 The bronze objects that King Solomon had made for the Temple—the two columns
2Kings25:17 The two columns were identical: each one was 27 feet high
2Kings25:18 In addition
2Kings25:19 From the city he took the officer who had been in command of the troops
2Kings25:20 Nebuzaradan took them to the king of Babylonia
2Kings25:21 in the territory of Hamath. There the king had them beaten and put to death. So the people of Judah were carried away from their land into exile.
2Kings25:22 King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylonia made Gedaliah
2Kings25:23 When the Judean officers and soldiers who had not surrendered heard about this
2Kings25:24 Gedaliah said to them
2Kings25:25 But in the seventh month of that year
2Kings25:26 Then all the Israelites
2Kings25:27 In the year that Evil-merodach became king of Babylonia
2Kings25:28 Evil-merodach treated him kindly and gave him a position of greater honor than he gave the other kings who were exiles with him in Babylonia.
2Kings25:29 So Jehoiachin was permitted to change from his prison clothes and to dine at the king's table for the rest of his life.

2Chronicles3:17 The columns were set at the sides of the Temple entrance: the one on the south side was named Jachin and the one on the north side was named Boaz.

2Chronicles8:8

2Chronicles9:1 The queen of Sheba heard of King Solomon's fame
2Chronicles9:2 He answered them all; there was nothing too difficult for him to explain.
2Chronicles9:3 The queen of Sheba heard Solomon's wisdom and saw the palace he had built.
2Chronicles9:4 She saw the food that was served at his table
2Chronicles9:5 She said to the king
2Chronicles9:6 I did not believe what they told me until I came and saw for myself. I had not heard of even half your wisdom. You are even wiser than people say.
2Chronicles9:7 How fortunate are those who serve you
2Chronicles9:8 Praise the Lord your God! He has shown how pleased he is with you by making you king
2Chronicles9:9 She presented to King Solomon the gifts she had brought: almost five tons of gold and a very large amount of spices and jewels. There have never been any other spices as fine as those that the queen of Sheba gave to King Solomon. (
2Chronicles9:10 The sailors of King Hiram and of King Solomon who brought gold from Ophir also brought juniper wood and jewels.
2Chronicles9:11 Solomon used the wood to make stairs for the Temple and for his palace
2Chronicles9:12 King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba everything she asked for. This was in addition to what he gave her in exchange for the gifts she brought to him. Then she and her attendants returned to the land of Sheba.
2Chronicles9:13 Every year King Solomon received over twenty-five tons of gold
2Chronicles9:14 in addition to the taxes paid by the traders and merchants. The kings of Arabia and the governors of the Israelite districts also brought him silver and gold.
2Chronicles9:15 Solomon made two hundred large shields
2Chronicles9:16 and three hundred smaller shields
2Chronicles9:17 The king also had a large throne made. Part of it was covered with ivory and the rest of it was covered with pure gold.
2Chronicles9:18 Six steps led up to the throne
2Chronicles9:19 Twelve figures of lions were on the steps
2Chronicles9:20 All of King Solomon's drinking cups were made of gold
2Chronicles9:21 He had a fleet of ocean-going ships sailing with King Hiram's fleet. Every three years his fleet would return
2Chronicles9:22 King Solomon was richer and wiser than any other king in the world.
2Chronicles9:23 They all consulted him
2Chronicles9:24 Each of them brought Solomon gifts—articles of silver and gold
2Chronicles9:25 King Solomon also had four thousand stalls for his chariots and horses
2Chronicles9:26 He was supreme ruler of all the kings in the territory from the Euphrates River to Philistia and the Egyptian border.
2Chronicles9:27 During his reign silver was as common in Jerusalem as stone
2Chronicles9:28 Solomon imported horses from Musri and from every other country.

2Chronicles9:31 He died and was buried in David's City

2Chronicles14:1 King Abijah died and was buried in the royal tombs in David's City. His son Asa succeeded him as king
2Chronicles14:2 Asa pleased the Lord
2Chronicles14:3 He removed the foreign altars and the pagan places of worship
2Chronicles14:4 He commanded the people of Judah to do the will of the Lord
2Chronicles14:5 Because he abolished the pagan places of worship and the incense altars from all the cities of Judah
2Chronicles14:6 He built fortifications for the cities of Judah during this time
2Chronicles14:7 He told the people of Judah
2Chronicles14:8 King Asa had an army of 300
2Chronicles14:9 An Ethiopian named Zerah invaded Judah with an army of a million men and three hundred chariots and advanced as far as Mareshah.
2Chronicles14:10 Asa went out to fight him
2Chronicles14:11 Asa prayed to the Lord his God
2Chronicles14:12 The Lord defeated the Ethiopian army when Asa and the Judean army attacked them. They fled
2Chronicles14:13 and Asa and his troops pursued them as far as Gerar. So many of the Ethiopians were killed that the army was unable to rally and fight. They were overpowered by the Lord and his army
2Chronicles14:14 Then they were able to destroy the cities in the area around Gerar
2Chronicles14:15 They also attacked the camps of some shepherds

2Chronicles15:2 and he went to meet King Asa. He called out

2Chronicles15:4 But when trouble came
2Chronicles15:5 In those days no one could come and go in safety
2Chronicles15:6 One nation oppressed another nation
2Chronicles15:7 But you must be strong and not be discouraged. The work that you do will be rewarded.”
2Chronicles15:8 When Asa heard the prophecy that Azariah son of Oded had spoken
2Chronicles15:9 Many people had come over to Asa's side from Ephraim
2Chronicles15:10 They assembled in Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year that Asa was king.
2Chronicles15:11 On that day they offered sacrifices to the Lord from the loot they had brought back: seven hundred head of cattle and seven thousand sheep.
2Chronicles15:12 They made a covenant in which they agreed to worship the Lord
2Chronicles15:13 Anyone
2Chronicles15:14 In a loud voice they took an oath in the Lord 's name that they would keep the covenant
2Chronicles15:15 All the people of Judah were happy because they had made this covenant with all their heart. They took delight in worshiping the Lord
2Chronicles15:16 King Asa removed his grandmother Maacah from her position as queen mother
2Chronicles15:17 Even though Asa did not destroy all the pagan places of worship in the land
2Chronicles15:18 He placed in the Temple all the objects his father Abijah had dedicated to God
2Chronicles15:19 There was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of his reign.

2Chronicles17:2 He stationed troops in the fortified cities of Judah
2Chronicles17:3 The Lord blessed Jehoshaphat because he followed the example of his father's early life and did not worship Baal.
2Chronicles17:4 He served his father's God
2Chronicles17:5 The Lord gave Jehoshaphat firm control over the kingdom of Judah
2Chronicles17:6 He took pride in serving the Lord and destroyed all the pagan places of worship and the symbols of the goddess Asherah in Judah.

Ezra8:10 I would have been ashamed to ask the emperor for a troop of cavalry to guard us from any enemies during our journey
Ezra8:11 So we fasted and prayed for God to protect us
Ezra8:12 From among the leading priests I chose Sherebiah
Ezra8:13 Then I weighed out the silver
Ezra8:14 This is what I gave them: silver - 25 tons 100 silver utensils - 150 pounds gold - 7
Ezra8:15 I said to them
Ezra8:16 Guard them carefully until you reach the Temple. There in the priests' rooms weigh them and turn them over to the leaders of the priests and of the Levites
Ezra8:17 So the priests and the Levites took charge of the silver
Ezra8:18 It was on the twelfth day of the first month that we left the Ahava Canal to go to Jerusalem. Our God was with us and protected us from enemy attacks and from ambush as we traveled.
Ezra8:19 When we reached Jerusalem
Ezra8:20 Then on the fourth day we went to the Temple
Ezra8:21 Everything was counted and weighed

Ezra9:14 Then how can we ignore your commandments again and intermarry with these wicked people? If we do

Nehemiah1:1 This is the account of what Nehemiah son of Hacaliah accomplished. In the month of Kislev in the twentieth year that Artaxerxes was emperor of Persia
Nehemiah1:2 Hanani
Nehemiah1:3 They told me that those who had survived and were back in the homeland were in great difficulty and that the foreigners who lived nearby looked down on them. They also told me that the walls of Jerusalem were still broken down and that the gates had not been restored since the time they were burned.
Nehemiah1:4 When I heard all this
Nehemiah1:5 “ Lord God of Heaven! You are great
Nehemiah1:6 Look at me
Nehemiah1:7 We have acted wickedly against you and have not done what you commanded. We have not kept the laws which you gave us through Moses
Nehemiah1:8 Remember now what you told Moses: ‘If you people of Israel are unfaithful to me
Nehemiah1:9 But then if you turn back to me and do what I have commanded you
Nehemiah1:10 “Lord
Nehemiah1:11 Listen now to my prayer and to the prayers of all your other servants who want to honor you. Give me success today and make the emperor merciful to me.” In those days I was the emperor's wine steward.

Nehemiah4:1 When Sanballat heard that we Jews had begun rebuilding the wall
Nehemiah4:2 In front of his companions and the Samaritan troops he said

Nehemiah4:4 I prayed
Nehemiah4:5 Don't forgive the evil they do and don't forget their sins
Nehemiah4:6 So we went on rebuilding the wall
Nehemiah4:7 Sanballat
Nehemiah4:8 So they all plotted together to come and attack Jerusalem and create confusion
Nehemiah4:9 but we prayed to our God and kept men on guard against them day and night.
Nehemiah4:10 The people of Judah had a song they sang: “We grow weak carrying burdens; There's so much rubble to take away. How can we build the wall today?”
Nehemiah4:11 Our enemies thought we would not see them or know what was happening until they were already upon us
Nehemiah4:12 But time after time Jews who were living among our enemies came to warn us of the plans our enemies were making against us.
Nehemiah4:13 So I armed the people with swords
Nehemiah4:14 I saw that the people were worried
Nehemiah4:15 Our enemies heard that we had found out what they were plotting
Nehemiah4:16 From then on half of my men worked and half stood guard
Nehemiah4:17 who were rebuilding the wall. Even those who carried building materials worked with one hand and kept a weapon in the other
Nehemiah4:18 and everyone who was building kept a sword strapped to their waist. The man who was to sound the alarm on the bugle stayed with me.
Nehemiah4:19 I told the people and their officials and leaders

Nehemiah4:21 So every day

Nehemiah5:7 and decided to act. I denounced the leaders and officials of the people and told them

Nehemiah5:14 During all the twelve years that I was governor of the land of Judah
Nehemiah5:15 Every governor who had been in office before me had been a burden to the people and had demanded forty silver coins a day for food and wine. Even their servants had oppressed the people. But I acted differently
Nehemiah5:16 I put all my energy into rebuilding the wall and did not acquire any property. Everyone who worked for me joined in the rebuilding.
Nehemiah5:17 I regularly fed at my table a hundred and fifty of the Jewish people and their leaders

Nehemiah6:1 Sanballat
Nehemiah6:2 So Sanballat and Geshem sent me a message
Nehemiah6:3 I sent messengers to say to them
Nehemiah6:4 They sent me the same message four times
Nehemiah6:5 Then Sanballat sent one of his servants to me with a fifth message
Nehemiah6:6 It read: “Geshem tells me that a rumor is going around among the neighboring peoples that you and the Jewish people intend to revolt and that this is why you are rebuilding the wall. He also says you plan to make yourself king
Nehemiah6:7 and that you have arranged for some prophets to proclaim in Jerusalem that you are the king of Judah. His Majesty is certain to hear about this
Nehemiah6:8 I sent a reply to him: “Nothing of what you are saying is true. You have made it all up yourself.”
Nehemiah6:9 They were trying to frighten us into stopping work. I prayed
Nehemiah6:10 About this time I went to visit Shemaiah
Nehemiah6:11 I answered
Nehemiah6:12 When I thought it over
Nehemiah6:13 They hired him to frighten me into sinning
Nehemiah6:14 I prayed
Nehemiah6:15 After fifty-two days of work the entire wall was finished on the twenty-fifth day of the month of Elul.
Nehemiah6:16 When our enemies in the surrounding nations heard this
Nehemiah6:17 During all this time the Jewish leaders had been in correspondence with Tobiah.
Nehemiah6:18 Many people in Judah were on his side because of his Jewish father-in-law
Nehemiah6:19 People would talk in front of me about all the good deeds Tobiah had done and would tell him everything I said. And he kept sending me letters to try to frighten me.
Nehemiah7:1 And now the wall had been rebuilt
Nehemiah7:2 I put two men in charge of governing the city of Jerusalem: my brother Hanani and Hananiah
Nehemiah7:3 I told them not to have the gates of Jerusalem opened in the morning until well after sunrise and to have them closed and barred before the guards went off duty at sunset. I also told them to appoint guards from among the people who lived in Jerusalem and to assign some of them to specific posts and others to patrol the area around their own houses.
Nehemiah7:4 Jerusalem was a large city
Nehemiah7:5 God inspired me to assemble the people and their leaders and officials and to check their family records. I located the records of those who had first returned from captivity
Nehemiah7:6 Many of the exiles left the province of Babylon and returned to Jerusalem and Judah

Nehemiah9:1 On the twenty-fourth day of the same month the people of Israel gathered to fast in order to show sorrow for their sins. They had already separated themselves from all foreigners. They wore sackcloth and put dust on their heads as signs of grief. Then they stood and began to confess the sins that they and their ancestors had committed.
Nehemiah9:2 
Nehemiah9:3 For about three hours the Law of the Lord their God was read to them
Nehemiah9:4 There was a platform for the Levites
Nehemiah9:5 The following Levites gave a call to worship: Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 And then the people of Israel prayed this prayer: “You
Nehemiah9:7 You
Nehemiah9:8 You found that he was faithful to you
Nehemiah9:9 “You saw how our ancestors suffered in Egypt; you heard their call for help at the Red Sea.
Nehemiah9:10 You worked amazing miracles against the king
Nehemiah9:11 Through the sea you made a path for your people and led them through on dry ground. Those who pursued them drowned in deep water
Nehemiah9:12 With a cloud you led them in daytime
Nehemiah9:13 At Mount Sinai you came down from heaven; you spoke to your people and gave them good laws and sound teachings.
Nehemiah9:14 You taught them to keep your Sabbaths holy
Nehemiah9:15 “When they were hungry
Nehemiah9:16 But our ancestors grew proud and stubborn and refused to obey your commands.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to obey; they forgot all you did; they forgot the miracles you had performed. In their pride they chose a leader to take them back to slavery in Egypt. But you are a God who forgives; you are gracious and loving
Nehemiah9:18 They made an idol in the shape of a bull-calf and said it was the god who led them from Egypt! How much they insulted you
Nehemiah9:19 But you did not abandon them there in the desert
Nehemiah9:20 In your goodness you told them what they should do; you fed them manna and gave them water to drink.
Nehemiah9:21 Through forty years in the desert you provided all that they needed; their clothing never wore out
Nehemiah9:22 “You let them conquer nations and kingdoms
Nehemiah9:23 You gave them as many children as there are stars in the sky
Nehemiah9:24 They conquered the land of Canaan; you overcame the people living there. You gave your people the power to do as they pleased with the people and kings of Canaan.
Nehemiah9:25 Your people captured fortified cities
Nehemiah9:26 “But your people rebelled and disobeyed you; they turned their backs on your Law. They killed the prophets who warned them
Nehemiah9:27 so you let their enemies conquer and rule them. In their trouble they called to you for help
Nehemiah9:28 When peace returned
Nehemiah9:29 You warned them to obey your teachings
Nehemiah9:30 Year after year you patiently warned them. You inspired your prophets to speak
Nehemiah9:31 And yet
Nehemiah9:32 “O God
Nehemiah9:33 You have done right to punish us; you have been faithful
Nehemiah9:34 Our ancestors
Nehemiah9:35 With your blessing
Nehemiah9:36 And now we are slaves in the land that you gave us
Nehemiah9:37 What the land produces goes to the kings that you put over us because we sinned. They do as they please with us and our livestock
Nehemiah9:38 Because of all that has happened

Job41:24 His stony heart is without fear
Job41:25 When he rises up
Job41:26 There is no sword that can wound him; no spear or arrow or lance that can harm him.
Job41:27 For him iron is as flimsy as straw
Job41:28 There is no arrow that can make him run; rocks thrown at him are like bits of straw.
Job41:29 To him a club is a piece of straw
Job41:30 The scales on his belly are like jagged pieces of pottery; they tear up the muddy ground like a threshing sledge.
Job41:31 He churns up the sea like boiling water and makes it bubble like a pot of oil.
Job41:32 He leaves a shining path behind him and turns the sea to white foam.
Job41:33 There is nothing on earth to compare with him; he is a creature that has no fear.
Job41:34 He looks down on even the proudest animals; he is king of all wild beasts.

Psalms10:4 The wicked do not care about the Lord; in their pride they think that God doesn't matter.
Psalms10:5 The wicked succeed in everything. They cannot understand God's judgments; they sneer at their enemies.

Psalms10:7 Their speech is filled with curses

Psalms78:34 Whenever he killed some of them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their protector
Psalms78:36 But their words were all lies; nothing they said was sincere.
Psalms78:37 They were not loyal to him; they were not faithful to their covenant with him.
Psalms78:38 But God was merciful to his people. He forgave their sin and did not destroy them. Many times he held back his anger and restrained his fury.
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were only mortal beings
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against him in the desert; how many times they made him sad!

Psalms78:42 They forgot his great power and the day when he saved them from their enemies

Psalms78:45 He sent flies among them
Psalms78:46 He sent locusts to eat their crops and to destroy their fields.

Psalms78:48 He killed their cattle with hail and their flocks with lightning.
Psalms78:49 He caused them great distress by pouring out his anger and fierce rage
Psalms78:50 He did not restrain his anger or spare their lives
Psalms78:51 He killed the first-born sons of all the families of Egypt.

Psalms78:53 He led them safely
Psalms78:54 He brought them to his holy land

Psalms78:59 God was angry when he saw it

Psalms78:61 He allowed our enemies to capture the Covenant Box

Psalms78:64 Priests died by violence

Psalms106:38 They killed those innocent children

Psalms106:40 So the Lord was angry with his people; he was disgusted with them.

Psalms107:8 They must thank the Lord for his constant love
Psalms107:9 He satisfies those who are thirsty and fills the hungry with good things.
Psalms107:10 Some were living in gloom and darkness
Psalms107:11 because they had rebelled against the commands of Almighty God and had rejected his instructions.
Psalms107:12 They were worn out from hard work; they would fall down

Psalms107:16 He breaks down doors of bronze and smashes iron bars.

Psalms107:29 He calmed the raging storm
Psalms107:30 They were glad because of the calm
Psalms107:31 They must thank the Lord for his constant love

Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon
Proverbs1:2 Here are proverbs that will help you recognize wisdom and good advice
Proverbs1:3 They can teach you how to live intelligently and how to be honest
Proverbs1:4 They can make an inexperienced person clever and teach young people how to be resourceful.
Proverbs1:5 These proverbs can even add to the knowledge of the wise and give guidance to the educated
Proverbs1:6 so that they can understand the hidden meanings of proverbs and the problems that the wise raise.
Proverbs1:7 To have knowledge
Proverbs1:8 My child
Proverbs1:9 Their teaching will improve your character as a handsome turban or a necklace improves your appearance.
Proverbs1:10 My child
Proverbs1:11 Suppose they say
Proverbs1:12 They may be alive and well when we find them
Proverbs1:13 We'll find all kinds of riches and fill our houses with loot!
Proverbs1:14 Come and join us
Proverbs1:15 My child
Proverbs1:16 They can't wait to do something bad. They're always ready to kill.
Proverbs1:17 It does no good to spread a net when the bird you want to catch is watching
Proverbs1:18 but people like that are setting a trap for themselves
Proverbs1:19 Robbery always claims the life of the robber—this is what happens to anyone who lives by violence.
Proverbs1:20 Listen! Wisdom is calling out in the streets and marketplaces
Proverbs1:21 calling loudly at the city gates and wherever people come together:
Proverbs1:22 “Foolish people! How long do you want to be foolish? How long will you enjoy making fun of knowledge? Will you never learn?
Proverbs1:23 Listen when I reprimand you; I will give you good advice and share my knowledge with you.
Proverbs1:24 I have been calling you
Proverbs1:25 You have ignored all my advice and have not been willing to let me correct you.
Proverbs1:26 So when you get into trouble
Proverbs1:27 when it comes on you like a storm
Proverbs1:28 Then you will call for wisdom
Proverbs1:29 You have never had any use for knowledge and have always refused to obey the Lord.
Proverbs1:30 You have never wanted my advice or paid any attention when I corrected you.
Proverbs1:31 So then
Proverbs1:32 Inexperienced people die because they reject wisdom. Stupid people are destroyed by their own lack of concern.
Proverbs1:33 But whoever listens to me will have security. He will be safe
Proverbs2:1 My child
Proverbs2:2 Listen to what is wise and try to understand it.
Proverbs2:3 Yes
Proverbs2:4 Look for it as hard as you would for silver or some hidden treasure.
Proverbs2:5 If you do
Proverbs2:6 It is the Lord who gives wisdom; from him come knowledge and understanding.
Proverbs2:7 He provides help and protection for those who are righteous and honest.
Proverbs2:8 He protects those who treat others fairly
Proverbs2:9 If you listen to me
Proverbs2:10 You will become wise
Proverbs2:11 Your insight and understanding will protect you
Proverbs2:12 and prevent you from doing the wrong thing. They will keep you away from people who stir up trouble by what they say—
Proverbs2:13 those who have abandoned a righteous life to live in the darkness of sin
Proverbs2:14 those who find pleasure in doing wrong and who enjoy senseless evil
Proverbs2:15 unreliable people who cannot be trusted.
Proverbs2:16 You will be able to resist any immoral woman who tries to seduce you with her smooth talk
Proverbs2:17 who is faithless to her own husband and forgets her sacred vows.
Proverbs2:18 If you go to her house
Proverbs2:19 No one who visits her ever comes back. He never returns to the road to life.
Proverbs2:20 So you must follow the example of good people and live a righteous life.
Proverbs2:21 Righteous people—people of integrity—will live in this land of ours.
Proverbs2:22 But God will snatch the wicked from the land and pull sinners out of it like plants from the ground.

Proverbs3:13 Happy is anyone who becomes wise—who comes to have understanding.
Proverbs3:14 There is more profit in it than there is in silver; it is worth more to you than gold.
Proverbs3:15 Wisdom is more valuable than jewels; nothing you could want can compare with it.
Proverbs3:16 Wisdom offers you long life
Proverbs3:17 Wisdom can make your life pleasant and lead you safely through it.
Proverbs3:18 Those who become wise are happy; wisdom will give them life.
Proverbs3:19 The Lord created the earth by his wisdom; by his knowledge he set the sky in place.
Proverbs3:20 His wisdom caused the rivers to flow and the clouds to give rain to the earth.
Proverbs3:21 My child
Proverbs3:22 They will provide you with life—a pleasant and happy life.
Proverbs3:23 You can go safely on your way and never even stumble.
Proverbs3:24 You will not be afraid when you go to bed
Proverbs3:25 You will not have to worry about sudden disasters
Proverbs3:26 The Lord will keep you safe. He will not let you fall into a trap.
Proverbs3:27 Whenever you possibly can
Proverbs3:28 Never tell your neighbors to wait until tomorrow if you can help them now.
Proverbs3:29 Don't plan anything that will hurt your neighbors; they live beside you
Proverbs3:30 Don't argue with others for no reason when they have never done you any harm.
Proverbs3:31 Don't be jealous of violent people or decide to act as they do
Proverbs3:32 because the Lord hates people who do evil
Proverbs3:33 The Lord puts a curse on the homes of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 He has no use for conceited people
Proverbs3:35 Wise people will gain an honorable reputation
Proverbs4:1 My children
Proverbs4:2 What I am teaching you is good
Proverbs4:3 When I was only a little boy
Proverbs4:4 my father would teach me. He would say
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom and insight! Do not forget or ignore what I say.
Proverbs4:6 Do not abandon wisdom
Proverbs4:7 Getting wisdom is the most important thing you can do. Whatever else you get
Proverbs4:8 Love wisdom
Proverbs4:9 She will be your crowning glory.”
Proverbs4:10 Listen to me
Proverbs4:11 I have taught you wisdom and the right way to live.
Proverbs4:12 Nothing will stand in your way if you walk wisely
Proverbs4:13 Always remember what you have learned. Your education is your life—guard it well.
Proverbs4:14 Do not go where evil people go. Do not follow the example of the wicked.
Proverbs4:15 Don't do it! Keep away from evil! Refuse it and go on your way.
Proverbs4:16 Wicked people cannot sleep unless they have done something wrong. They lie awake unless they have hurt someone.
Proverbs4:17 Wickedness and violence are like food and drink to them.
Proverbs4:18 The road the righteous travel is like the sunrise
Proverbs4:19 The road of the wicked
Proverbs4:20 My child
Proverbs4:21 Never let them get away from you. Remember them and keep them in your heart.
Proverbs4:22 They will give life and health to anyone who understands them.
Proverbs4:23 Be careful how you think; your life is shaped by your thoughts.
Proverbs4:24 Never say anything that isn't true. Have nothing to do with lies and misleading words.
Proverbs4:25 Look straight ahead with honest confidence; don't hang your head in shame.
Proverbs4:26 Plan carefully what you do
Proverbs4:27 Avoid evil and walk straight ahead. Don't go one step off the right way.

Proverbs5:4 but when it is all over

Proverbs5:7 Now listen to me
Proverbs5:8 Keep away from such a woman! Don't even go near her door!
Proverbs5:9 If you do

Proverbs5:12 and you will say

Proverbs5:14 And suddenly I found myself publicly disgraced.”
Proverbs5:15 Be faithful to your own wife and give your love to her alone.
Proverbs5:16 Children that you have by other women will do you no good.
Proverbs5:17 Your children should grow up to help you
Proverbs5:18 So be happy with your wife and find your joy with the woman you married—
Proverbs5:19 pretty and graceful as a deer. Let her charms keep you happy; let her surround you with her love.
Proverbs5:20 Son
Proverbs5:21 The Lord sees everything you do. Wherever you go
Proverbs5:22 The sins of the wicked are a trap. They get caught in the net of their own sin.
Proverbs5:23 They die because they have no self-control. Their utter stupidity will send them to their graves.
Proverbs6:1 My child
Proverbs6:2 Have you been caught by your own words
Proverbs6:3 Well then
Proverbs6:4 Don't let yourself go to sleep or even stop to rest.
Proverbs6:5 Get out of the trap like a bird or a deer escaping from a hunter.
Proverbs6:6 Lazy people should learn a lesson from the way ants live.

Isaiah43:19 Watch for the new thing I am going to do. It is happening already—you can see it now! I will make a road through the wilderness and give you streams of water there.
Isaiah43:20 Even the wild animals will honor me; jackals and ostriches will praise me when I make rivers flow in the desert to give water to my chosen people.
Isaiah43:21 They are the people I made for myself
Isaiah43:22 The Lord says
Isaiah43:23 You did not bring me your burnt offerings of sheep; you did not honor me with your sacrifices. I did not burden you by demanding offerings or wear you out by asking for incense.
Isaiah43:24 You didn't buy incense for me or satisfy me with the fat of your animals. Instead you burdened me with your sins; you wore me out with the wrongs you have committed.
Isaiah43:25 And yet
Isaiah43:26 “Let us go to court; bring your accusation! Present your case to prove you are in the right!

Isaiah44:2 I am the Lord who created you; from the time you were born

Isaiah44:5 “One by one
Isaiah44:6 The Lord
Isaiah44:7 Could anyone else have done what I did? Who could have predicted all that would happen from the very beginning to the end of time?

Isaiah44:9 All those who make idols are worthless
Isaiah44:10 It does no good to make a metal image to worship as a god!
Isaiah44:11 Everyone who worships it will be humiliated. The people who make idols are human beings and nothing more. Let them come and stand trial—they will be terrified and will suffer disgrace.
Isaiah44:12 The metalworker takes a piece of metal and works with it over a fire. His strong arm swings a hammer to pound the metal into shape. As he works
Isaiah44:13 The carpenter measures the wood. He outlines a figure with chalk
Isaiah44:14 He might cut down cedars to use
Isaiah44:15 A person uses part of a tree for fuel and part of it for making an idol. With one part he builds a fire to warm himself and bake bread; with the other part he makes a god and worships it.
Isaiah44:16 With some of the wood he makes a fire; he roasts meat
Isaiah44:17 The rest of the wood he makes into an idol
Isaiah44:18 Such people are too stupid to know what they are doing. They close their eyes and their minds to the truth.
Isaiah44:19 The maker of idols hasn't the wit or the sense to say
Isaiah44:20 It makes as much sense as eating ashes. His foolish ideas have so misled him that he is beyond help. He won't admit to himself that the idol he holds in his hand is not a god at all.

Isaiah44:22 I have swept your sins away like a cloud. Come back to me; I am the one who saves you.”

Isaiah65:1 The Lord said
Isaiah65:2 I have always been ready to welcome my people
Isaiah65:3 They shamelessly keep on making me angry. They offer pagan sacrifices at sacred gardens and burn incense on pagan altars.
Isaiah65:4 At night they go to caves and tombs to consult the spirits of the dead. They eat pork and drink broth made from meat offered in pagan sacrifices.
Isaiah65:5 And then they say to others
Isaiah65:6 “I have already decided on their punishment
Isaiah65:7 for their sins and the sins of their ancestors. They have burned incense at pagan hill shrines and spoken evil of me. So I will punish them as their past deeds deserve.”

Isaiah65:16 Anyone in the land who asks for a blessing will ask to be blessed by the faithful God. Whoever takes an oath will swear by the name of the faithful God. The troubles of the past will be gone and forgotten.”

They take pleasure in disgusting ways of worship.

Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king of Judah
Jeremiah52:2 King Zedekiah sinned against the Lord
Jeremiah52:3 The Lord became so angry with the people of Jerusalem and Judah that he banished them from his sight. Zedekiah rebelled against King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylonia
Jeremiah52:4 and so Nebuchadnezzar came with all his army and attacked Jerusalem on the tenth day of the tenth month of the ninth year of Zedekiah's reign. They set up camp outside the city
Jeremiah52:5 and kept it under siege until Zedekiah's eleventh year.
Jeremiah52:6 On the ninth day of the fourth month of that same year
Jeremiah52:7 the city walls were broken through. Although the Babylonians were surrounding the city
Jeremiah52:8 But the Babylonian army pursued King Zedekiah
Jeremiah52:9 Zedekiah was taken to King Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:10 At Riblah he put Zedekiah's sons to death while Zedekiah was looking on and he also had the officials of Judah executed.
Jeremiah52:11 After that
Jeremiah52:12 On the tenth day of the fifth month of the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylonia
Jeremiah52:13 He burned down the Temple
Jeremiah52:14 and his soldiers tore down the city walls.
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan took away to Babylonia the people who were left in the city
Jeremiah52:16 But he left in Judah some of the poorest people
Jeremiah52:17 The Babylonians broke in pieces the bronze columns and the carts that were in the Temple
Jeremiah52:18 They also took away the shovels and the ash containers used in cleaning the altar
Jeremiah52:19 They took away everything that was made of gold or silver: the small bowls
Jeremiah52:20 The bronze objects that King Solomon had made for the Temple—the two columns
Jeremiah52:21 The two columns were identical: each one was 27 feet high and 18 feet around. They were hollow
Jeremiah52:22 
Jeremiah52:23 On the grillwork of each column there were a hundred pomegranates in all
Jeremiah52:24 In addition
Jeremiah52:25 From the city he took the officer who had been in command of the troops
Jeremiah52:26 Nebuzaradan took them to the king of Babylonia
Jeremiah52:27 in the territory of Hamath. There the king had them beaten and put to death. So the people of Judah were carried away from their land into exile.
Jeremiah52:28 This is the record of the people that Nebuchadnezzar took away as prisoners: in his seventh year as king he carried away 3
Jeremiah52:29 in his eighteenth year
Jeremiah52:30 and in his twenty-third year
Jeremiah52:31 In the year that Evil-merodach became king of Babylonia
Jeremiah52:32 Evil-merodach treated him kindly and gave him a position of greater honor than he gave the other kings who were exiles with him in Babylonia.
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin was permitted to change from his prison clothes and to dine at the king's table for the rest of his life.
Jeremiah52:34 Each day for as long as he lived
Lamentations1:1 How lonely lies Jerusalem
Lamentations1:2 All night long she cries; tears run down her cheeks. Of all her former friends
Lamentations1:3 Judah's people are helpless slaves
Lamentations1:4 No one comes to the Temple now to worship on the holy days. The young women who sang there suffer
Lamentations1:5 Her enemies succeeded; they hold her in their power. The Lord has made her suffer for all her many sins; Her children have been captured and taken away.
Lamentations1:6 The splendor of Jerusalem is a thing of the past. Her leaders are like deer that are weak from hunger
Lamentations1:7 A lonely ruin now
Lamentations1:8 Her honor is gone; she is naked and held in contempt. She groans and hides her face in shame. Jerusalem made herself filthy with terrible sin.
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness was easily seen
Lamentations1:10 The enemies robbed her of all her treasures. She saw them enter the Temple itself
Lamentations1:11 Her people groan as they look for something to eat; They exchange their treasures for food to keep themselves alive. “Look at me
Lamentations1:12 “Look at me!” she cries to everyone who passes by. “No one has ever had pain like mine
Lamentations1:13 “He sent fire from above
Lamentations1:14 “He took note of all my sins and tied them all together; He hung them around my neck
Lamentations1:15 “The Lord laughed at all my strongest soldiers; He sent an army to destroy my young men. He crushed my people like grapes in a wine press.
Lamentations1:16 “That is why my eyes are overflowing with tears. No one can comfort me; no one can give me courage. The enemy has conquered me; my people have nothing left.
Lamentations1:17 “I stretch out my hands
Lamentations1:18 “But the Lord is just
Lamentations1:19 “I called to my allies
Lamentations1:20 “Look
Lamentations1:21 “Listen to my groans; there is no one to comfort me. My enemies are glad that you brought disaster on me. Bring the day you promised; make my enemies suffer as I do.
Lamentations1:22 “Condemn them for all their wickedness; Punish them as you punished me for my sins. I groan in misery
Lamentations2:1 The Lord in his anger has covered Zion with darkness. Its heavenly splendor he has turned into ruins. On the day of his anger he abandoned even his Temple.
Lamentations2:2 The Lord destroyed without mercy every village in Judah And tore down the forts that defended the land. He brought disgrace on the kingdom and its rulers.
Lamentations2:3 In his fury he shattered the strength of Israel; He refused to help us when the enemy came. He raged against us like fire
Lamentations2:4 He aimed his arrows at us like an enemy; He killed all those who were our joy and delight. Here in Jerusalem we felt his burning anger.
Lamentations2:5 Like an enemy
Lamentations2:6 He smashed to pieces the Temple where we worshiped him; He has put an end to holy days and Sabbaths. King and priest alike have felt the force of his anger.
Lamentations2:7 The Lord rejected his altar and deserted his holy Temple; He allowed the enemy to tear down its walls. They shouted in victory where once we had worshiped in joy.
Lamentations2:8 The Lord was determined that the walls of Zion should fall; He measured them off to make sure of total destruction. The towers and walls now lie in ruins together.
Lamentations2:9 The gates lie buried in rubble
Lamentations2:10 Jerusalem's old men sit on the ground in silence
Lamentations2:11 My eyes are worn out with weeping; my soul is in anguish. I am exhausted with grief at the destruction of my people. Children and babies are fainting in the streets of the city.
Lamentations2:12 Hungry and thirsty
Lamentations2:13 O Jerusalem
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets had nothing to tell you but lies; Their preaching deceived you by never exposing your sin. They made you think you did not need to repent.
Lamentations2:15 People passing by the city look at you in scorn. They shake their heads and laugh at Jerusalem's ruins: “Is this that lovely city? Is this the pride of the world?”
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies mock you and glare at you with hate. They curl their lips and sneer
Lamentations2:17 The Lord has finally done what he threatened to do: He has destroyed us without mercy
Lamentations2:18 O Jerusalem
Lamentations2:19 All through the night get up again and again to cry out to the Lord; Pour out your heart and beg him for mercy on your children— Children starving to death on every street corner!
Lamentations2:20 Look
Lamentations2:21 Young and old alike lie dead in the streets
Lamentations2:22 You invited my enemies to hold a carnival of terror all around me
Lamentations3:1 I am one who knows what it is to be punished by God.
Lamentations3:2 He drove me deeper and deeper into darkness
Lamentations3:3 And beat me again and again with merciless blows.
Lamentations3:4 He has left my flesh open and raw
Lamentations3:5 He has shut me in a prison of misery and anguish.
Lamentations3:6 He has forced me to live in the stagnant darkness of death.
Lamentations3:7 He has bound me in chains; I am a prisoner with no hope of escape.
Lamentations3:8 I cry aloud for help
Lamentations3:9 I stagger as I walk; stone walls block me wherever I turn.
Lamentations3:10 He waited for me like a bear; he pounced on me like a lion.
Lamentations3:11 He chased me off the road
Lamentations3:12 He drew his bow and made me the target for his arrows.
Lamentations3:13 He shot his arrows deep into my body.
Lamentations3:14 People laugh at me all day long; I am a joke to them all.
Lamentations3:15 Bitter suffering is all he has given me for food and drink.
Lamentations3:16 He rubbed my face in the ground and broke my teeth on rocks.
Lamentations3:17 I have forgotten what health and peace and happiness are.
Lamentations3:18 I do not have much longer to live; my hope in the Lord is gone.
Lamentations3:19 The thought of my pain
Lamentations3:20 I think of it constantly
Lamentations3:21 Yet hope returns when I remember this one thing:
Lamentations3:22 The Lord 's unfailing love and mercy still continue
Lamentations3:23 Fresh as the morning
Lamentations3:24 The Lord is all I have
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is good to everyone who trusts in him
Lamentations3:26 So it is best for us to wait in patience—to wait for him to save us—
Lamentations3:27 And it is best to learn this patience in our youth.
Lamentations3:28 When we suffer
Lamentations3:29 We should bow in submission
Lamentations3:30 Though beaten and insulted
Lamentations3:31 The Lord is merciful and will not reject us forever.
Lamentations3:32 He may bring us sorrow
Lamentations3:33 He takes no pleasure in causing us grief or pain.
Lamentations3:34 The Lord knows when our spirits are crushed in prison;
Lamentations3:35 He knows when we are denied the rights he gave us;
Lamentations3:36 When justice is perverted in court
Lamentations3:37 The will of the Lord alone is always carried out.
Lamentations3:38 Good and evil alike take place at his command.
Lamentations3:39 Why should we ever complain when we are punished for our sin?
Lamentations3:40 Let us examine our ways and turn back to the Lord.
Lamentations3:41 Let us open our hearts to God in heaven and pray
Lamentations3:42 “We have sinned and rebelled
Lamentations3:43 “You pursued us and killed us; your mercy was hidden by your anger
Lamentations3:44 By a cloud of fury too thick for our prayers to get through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us the garbage dump of the world.
Lamentations3:46 “We are insulted and mocked by all our enemies.
Lamentations3:47 We have been through disaster and ruin; we live in danger and fear.
Lamentations3:48 My eyes flow with rivers of tears at the destruction of my people.
Lamentations3:49 “My tears will pour out in a ceaseless stream
Lamentations3:50 Until the Lord looks down from heaven and sees us.
Lamentations3:51 My heart is grieved when I see what has happened to the women of the city.
Lamentations3:52 “I was trapped like a bird by enemies who had no cause to hate me.
Lamentations3:53 They threw me alive into a pit and closed the opening with a stone.
Lamentations3:54 Water began to close over me
Lamentations3:55 “From the bottom of the pit
Lamentations3:56 And when I begged you to listen to my cry
Lamentations3:57 You answered me and told me not to be afraid.
Lamentations3:58 “You came to my rescue
Lamentations3:59 Judge in my favor; you know the wrongs done against me.
Lamentations3:60 You know how my enemies hate me and how they plot against me.
Lamentations3:61 “You have heard them insult me
Lamentations3:62 All day long they talk about me and make their plans.
Lamentations3:63 From morning till night they make fun of me.
Lamentations3:64 “Punish them for what they have done
Lamentations3:65 Curse them and fill them with despair!
Lamentations3:66 Hunt them down and wipe them off the earth!”
Lamentations4:1 Our glittering gold has grown dull; the stones of the Temple lie scattered in the streets.
Lamentations4:2 Zion's young people were as precious to us as gold
Lamentations4:3 Even a mother wolf will nurse her cubs
Lamentations4:4 They let their babies die of hunger and thirst; children are begging for food that no one will give them.
Lamentations4:5 People who once ate the finest foods die starving in the streets; those raised in luxury are pawing through garbage for food.
Lamentations4:6 My people have been punished even more than the inhabitants of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Our princes were undefiled and pure as snow
Lamentations4:8 Now they lie unknown in the streets
Lamentations4:9 Those who died in the war were better off than those who died later
Lamentations4:10 The disaster that came to my people brought horror; loving mothers boiled their own children for food.

Lamentations4:18 The enemy was watching for us; we could not even walk in the streets. Our days were over; the end had come.

Lamentations4:20 They captured the source of our life

Lamentations5:3 Our fathers have been killed by the enemy
Lamentations5:4 We must pay for the water we drink; we must buy the wood we need for fuel.

Lamentations5:6 To get food enough to stay alive

Lamentations5:8 Our rulers are no better than slaves
Lamentations5:9 Murderers roam through the countryside; we risk our lives when we look for food.

Ezekiel1:3 There in Babylonia beside the Chebar River
Ezekiel1:4 I looked up and saw a windstorm coming from the north. Lightning was flashing from a huge cloud
Ezekiel1:5 At the center of the storm I saw what looked like four living creatures in human form

Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight

Ezekiel1:25 but there was still a sound coming from above the dome over their heads.

Ezekiel40:1 It was the tenth day of the new year
Ezekiel40:2 In a vision God took me to the land of Israel and put me on a high mountain. I saw in front of me a group of buildings that looked like a city.
Ezekiel40:3 He took me closer
Ezekiel40:4 He said to me
Ezekiel40:5 What I saw was the Temple
Ezekiel40:6 Then he went to the gateway that faced east. He went up the steps
Ezekiel40:7 Beyond it there was a passageway
Ezekiel40:8 He measured this room and found it was 14 feet deep. It formed that end of the gateway which was nearest the Temple
Ezekiel40:9 
Ezekiel40:10 These guardrooms on each side of the passageway were all the same size
Ezekiel40:11 Next
Ezekiel40:12 In front of each of the guardrooms there was a low wall 20 inches high and 20 inches thick. (The rooms were 10 feet square.)

Daniel2:12 At that
Daniel2:13 So the order was issued for all of them to be killed

Daniel2:35 At once the iron
Daniel2:36 “This was the dream. Now I will tell Your Majesty what it means.
Daniel2:37 Your Majesty

Daniel2:39 After you there will be another empire
Daniel2:40 And then there will be a fourth empire

Daniel2:42 The toes—partly iron and partly clay—mean that part of the empire will be strong and part of it weak.
Daniel2:43 You also saw that the iron was mixed with the clay. This means that the rulers of that empire will try to unite their families by intermarriage
Daniel2:44 At the time of those rulers the God of heaven will establish a kingdom that will never end. It will never be conquered

Daniel5:2 While they were drinking
Daniel5:3 At once the gold cups and bowls were brought in
Daniel5:4 and praised gods made of gold
Daniel5:5 Suddenly a human hand appeared and began writing on the plaster wall of the palace
Daniel5:6 He turned pale and was so frightened that his knees began to shake.
Daniel5:7 He shouted for someone to bring in the magicians
Daniel5:8 The royal advisers came forward
Daniel5:9 In his distress King Belshazzar grew even paler
Daniel5:10 The queen mother heard the noise made by the king and his noblemen and entered the banquet hall. She said

Daniel5:12 He has unusual ability and is wise and skillful in interpreting dreams
Daniel5:13 Daniel was brought at once into the king's presence

Daniel5:15 The advisers and magicians were brought in to read this writing and tell me what it means
Daniel5:16 Now I have heard that you can find hidden meanings and explain mysteries. If you can read this writing and tell me what it means
Daniel5:17 Daniel replied

Daniel7:1 In the first year that Belshazzar was king of Babylonia
Daniel7:2 of what I saw that night: Winds were blowing from all directions and lashing the surface of the ocean.
Daniel7:3 Four huge beasts came up out of the ocean
Daniel7:4 The first one looked like a lion
Daniel7:5 The second beast looked like a bear standing on its hind legs. It was holding three ribs between its teeth
Daniel7:6 While I was watching
Daniel7:7 As I was watching
Daniel7:8 While I was staring at the horns
Daniel7:9 While I was looking
Daniel7:10 and a stream of fire was pouring out from it. There were many thousands of people there to serve him

Daniel7:20 And I wanted to know about the ten horns on its head and the horn that had come up afterward and had made three of the horns fall. It had eyes and a mouth and was boasting proudly. It was more terrifying than any of the others.
Daniel7:21 While I was looking
Daniel7:22 Then the one who had been living forever came and pronounced judgment in favor of the people of the Supreme God. The time had arrived for God's people to receive royal power.
Daniel7:23 This is the explanation I was given: “The fourth beast is a fourth empire that will be on the earth and will be different from all other empires. It will crush the whole earth and trample it down.
Daniel7:24 The ten horns are ten kings who will rule that empire. Then another king will appear; he will be very different from the earlier ones and will overthrow three kings.
Daniel7:25 He will speak against the Supreme God and oppress God's people. He will try to change their religious laws and festivals
Daniel7:26 Then the heavenly court will sit in judgment
Daniel7:27 The power and greatness of all the kingdoms on earth will be given to the people of the Supreme God. Their royal power will never end
Daniel7:28 This is the end of the account. I was so frightened that I turned pale
Daniel8:1 In the third year that Belshazzar was king
Daniel8:2 In the vision I suddenly found myself in the walled city of Susa in the province of Elam. I was standing by the Ulai River
Daniel8:3 and there beside the river I saw a ram that had two long horns
Daniel8:4 I watched the ram butting with his horns to the west
Daniel8:5 While I was wondering what this meant
Daniel8:6 He came toward the ram
Daniel8:7 I watched him attack the ram. He was so angry that he smashed into him and broke the two horns. The ram had no strength to resist. He was thrown to the ground and trampled on
Daniel8:8 The goat grew more and more arrogant
Daniel8:9 Out of one of these four horns grew a little horn
Daniel8:10 It grew strong enough to attack the army of heaven
Daniel8:11 It even defied the Prince of the heavenly army
Daniel8:12 People sinned there instead of offering the proper daily sacrifices
Daniel8:13 Then I heard one angel ask another
Daniel8:14 I heard the other angel answer
Daniel8:15 I was trying to understand what the vision meant

Daniel8:18 While he was talking

Daniel8:21 The goat represents the kingdom of Greece
Daniel8:22 The four horns that came up when the first horn was broken represent the four kingdoms into which that nation will be divided and which will not be as strong as the first kingdom.

Daniel8:24 He will grow strong—but not by his own power. He will cause terrible destruction and be successful in everything he does. He will bring destruction on powerful men and on God's own people.
Daniel8:25 Because he is cunning
Daniel8:26 This vision about the evening and morning sacrifices which has been explained to you will come true. But keep it secret now
Daniel8:27 I was depressed and ill for several days. Then I got up and went back to the work that the king had assigned to me
