Hosea12:1 Everything that the people of Israel do from morning to night is useless and destructive. Treachery and acts of violence increase among them. They make treaties with Assyria and do business with Egypt.”
Hosea12:2 The Lord has an accusation to bring against the people of Judah; he is also going to punish Israel for the way her people act. He will pay them back for what they have done.
Hosea12:3 Their ancestor Jacob struggled with his twin brother Esau while the two of them were still in their mother's womb; when Jacob grew up
Hosea12:4 he fought against an angel and won. He wept and asked for a blessing. And at Bethel God came to our ancestor Jacob and spoke with him.
Hosea12:5 This was the Lord God Almighty—the Lord is the name by which he is to be worshiped.
Hosea12:6 So now
Hosea12:7 The Lord says
Hosea12:8 ‘We are rich
Hosea12:9 But I
Hosea12:10 “I spoke to the prophets and gave them many visions
Hosea12:11 Yet idols are worshiped in Gilead
Hosea12:12 Our ancestor Jacob had to flee to Mesopotamia
Hosea12:13 The Lord sent a prophet to rescue the people of Israel from slavery in Egypt and to take care of them.
Hosea12:14 The people of Israel have made the Lord bitterly angry; they deserve death for their crimes. Their Lord will punish them for the disgrace they have brought on him.
Hosea13:1 In the past
Hosea13:2 They still keep on sinning by making metal images to worship—idols of silver
Hosea13:3 And so these people will disappear like morning mist
Hosea13:4 The Lord says
Hosea13:5 I took care of you in a dry
Hosea13:6 But when you entered the good land
Hosea13:7 So I will attack you like a lion. Like a leopard I will lie in wait along your path.
Hosea13:8 I will attack you like a bear that has lost her cubs
Hosea13:9 “I will destroy you
Hosea13:10 You asked for a king and for leaders
Hosea13:11 In my anger I have given you kings
Hosea13:12 “Israel's sin and guilt are on record
Hosea13:13 Israel has a chance to live
Hosea13:14 I will not save this people from the world of the dead or rescue them from the power of death. Bring on your plagues
Hosea13:15 Even though Israel flourishes like weeds
Hosea13:16 Samaria must be punished for rebelling against me. Her people will die in war; babies will be dashed to the ground
Hosea14:1 Return to the Lord your God
Hosea14:2 Return to the Lord
Hosea14:3 Assyria can never save us
Hosea14:4 The Lord says
Hosea14:5 I will be to the people of Israel like rain in a dry land. They will blossom like flowers; they will be firmly rooted like the trees of Lebanon.
Hosea14:6 They will be alive with new growth
Hosea14:7 Once again they will live under my protection. They will grow crops of grain and be fruitful like a vineyard. They will be as famous as the wine of Lebanon.
Hosea14:8 The people of Israel will have nothing more to do with idols; I will answer their prayers and take care of them. Like an evergreen tree I will shelter them; I am the source of all their blessings.”
Hosea14:9 May those who are wise understand what is written here
Joel1:1 This is the Lord 's message to Joel son of Pethuel.
Joel1:2 Pay attention
Joel1:3 Tell your children about it; they will tell their children
Joel1:4 Swarm after swarm of locusts settled on the crops; what one swarm left
Joel1:5 Wake up and weep
Joel1:6 An army of locusts has attacked our land; they are powerful and too many to count; their teeth are as sharp as those of a lion.
Joel1:7 They have destroyed our grapevines and chewed up our fig trees. They have stripped off the bark
Joel1:8 Cry
Joel1:9 There is no grain or wine to offer in the Temple; the priests mourn because they have no offerings for the Lord.
Joel1:10 The fields are bare; the ground mourns because the grain is destroyed
Joel1:11 Grieve
Joel1:12 The grapevines and fig trees have withered; all the fruit trees have wilted and died. The joy of the people is gone.
Joel1:13 Put on sackcloth and weep
Joel1:14 Give orders for a fast; call an assembly! Gather the leaders and all the people of Judah into the Temple of the Lord your God and cry out to him!
Joel1:15 The day of the Lord is near
Joel1:16 We look on helpless as our crops are destroyed. There is no joy in the Temple of our God.
Joel1:17 The seeds die in the dry earth. There is no grain to be stored
Joel1:18 The cattle are bellowing in distress because there is no pasture for them; the flocks of sheep also suffer.
Joel1:19 I cry out to you
Joel1:20 Even the wild animals cry out to you because the streams have become dry.
Joel2:1 Blow the trumpet; sound the alarm on Zion
Joel2:2 It will be a dark and gloomy day
Joel2:3 Like fire they eat up the plants. In front of them the land is like the Garden of Eden
Joel2:4 They look like horses; they run like war-horses.
Joel2:5 As they leap on the tops of the mountains
Joel2:6 As they approach
Joel2:7 They attack like warriors; they climb the walls like soldiers. They all keep marching straight ahead and do not change direction
Joel2:8 or get in each other's way. They swarm through defenses
Joel2:9 They rush against the city; they run over the walls; they climb up the houses and go in through the windows like thieves.
Joel2:10 The earth shakes as they advance; the sky trembles. The sun and the moon grow dark
Joel2:11 The Lord thunders commands to his army. The troops that obey him are many and mighty. How terrible is the day of the Lord! Who will survive it?
Joel2:12 “But even now
Joel2:13 Let your broken heart show your sorrow; tearing your clothes is not enough.” Come back to the Lord your God. He is kind and full of mercy; he is patient and keeps his promise; he is always ready to forgive and not punish.
Joel2:14 Perhaps the Lord your God will change his mind and bless you with abundant crops. Then you can offer him grain and wine.
Joel2:15 Blow the trumpet on Mount Zion; give orders for a fast and call an assembly!
Joel2:16 Gather the people together; prepare them for a sacred meeting; bring the old people; gather the children and the babies too. Even newly married couples must leave their homes and come.
Joel2:17 The priests
Joel2:18 Then the Lord showed concern for his land; he had mercy on his people.
Joel2:19 He answered them: “Now I am going to give you grain and wine and olive oil
Joel2:20 I will remove the locust army that came from the north and will drive some of them into the desert. Their front ranks will be driven into the Dead Sea
Joel2:21 “Fields
Joel2:22 Animals
Joel2:23 “Be glad
Joel2:24 The threshing places will be full of grain; the pits beside the presses will overflow with wine and olive oil.
Joel2:25 I will give you back what you lost in the years when swarms of locusts ate your crops. It was I who sent this army against you.
Joel2:26 Now you will have plenty to eat
Joel2:27 Then
Joel2:28 “Afterward I will pour out my Spirit on everyone: your sons and daughters will proclaim my message; your old people will have dreams
Joel2:29 At that time I will pour out my Spirit even on servants
Joel2:30 “I will give warnings of that day in the sky and on the earth; there will be bloodshed
Joel2:31 The sun will be darkened
Joel2:32 But all who ask the Lord for help will be saved. As the Lord has said
Joel3:1 The Lord says
Joel3:2 I will gather all the nations and bring them to the Valley of Judgment. There I will judge them for all they have done to my people. They have scattered the Israelites in foreign countries and divided Israel
Joel3:3 They threw dice to decide who would get the captives. They sold boys and girls into slavery to pay for prostitutes and wine.
Joel3:4 “What are you trying to do to me
Joel3:5 You have taken my silver and gold and carried my rich treasures into your temples.
Joel3:6 You have taken the people of Judah and Jerusalem far from their own country and sold them to the Greeks.
Joel3:7 Now I am going to bring them out of the places to which you have sold them. I will do to you what you have done to them.
Joel3:8 I will let your sons and daughters be sold to the people of Judah; they will sell them to the far-off Sabeans. I
Joel3:9 “Make this announcement among the nations: ‘Prepare for war; call your warriors; gather all your soldiers and march!
Joel3:10 Hammer the points of your plows into swords and your pruning knives into spears. Even the weak must fight.
Joel3:11 Hurry and come
Joel3:12 “The nations must get ready and come to the Valley of Judgment. There I
Joel3:13 They are very wicked; cut them down like grain at harvest time; crush them as grapes are crushed in a full wine press until the wine runs over.”
Joel3:14 Thousands and thousands are in the Valley of Judgment. It is there that the day of the Lord will soon come.
Joel3:15 The sun and the moon grow dark
Joel3:16 The Lord roars from Mount Zion; his voice thunders from Jerusalem; earth and sky tremble. But he will defend his people.
Joel3:17 “Then
Joel3:18 At that time the mountains will be covered with vineyards
Joel3:19 “Egypt will become a desert
Joel3:20 I will avenge those who were killed; I will not spare the guilty. But Judah and Jerusalem will be inhabited forever

Amos2:8 At every place of worship people sleep on clothing that they have taken from the poor as security for debts. In the temple of their God they drink wine which they have taken from those who owe them money.
Amos2:9 “And yet

Amos2:15 Archers will not stand their ground

Amos3:1 People of Israel
Amos3:2 “Of all the nations on earth
Amos3:3 Do two people start traveling together without arranging to meet?
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the forest unless he has found a victim? Does a young lion growl in his den unless he has caught something?
Amos3:5 Does a bird get caught in a trap if the trap has not been baited? Does a trap spring unless something sets it off?
Amos3:6 Does the war trumpet sound in a city without making the people afraid? Does disaster strike a city unless the Lord sends it?
Amos3:7 The Sovereign Lord never does anything without revealing his plan to his servants
Amos3:8 When a lion roars
Amos3:9 Announce to those who live in the palaces of Egypt and Ashdod: “Gather together in the hills around Samaria and see the great disorder and the crimes being committed there.”

Amos3:11 And so an enemy will surround their land

Amos4:2 As the Sovereign Lord is holy
Amos4:3 You will be dragged to the nearest break in the wall and thrown out.”
Amos4:4 The Sovereign Lord says
Amos4:5 Go on and offer your bread in thanksgiving to God
Amos4:6 “I was the one who brought famine to all your cities
Amos4:7 I kept it from raining when your crops needed it most. I sent rain on one city
Amos4:8 Weak with thirst
Amos4:9 “I sent a scorching wind to dry up your crops. The locusts ate up all your gardens and vineyards
Amos4:10 “I sent a plague on you like the one I sent on Egypt. I killed your young men in battle and took your horses away. I filled your nostrils with the stink of dead bodies in your camps. Still you did not come back to me.
Amos4:11 “I destroyed some of you as I destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah. Those of you who survived were like a burning stick saved from a fire. Still you did not come back to me
Amos4:12 “So then
Amos4:13 God is the one who made the mountains and created the winds. He makes his thoughts known to people; he changes day into night. He walks on the heights of the earth. This is his name: the Lord God Almighty!

Amos5:13 And so

Amos5:19 It will be like someone who runs from a lion and meets a bear! Or like someone who comes home and puts his hand on the wall—only to be bitten by a snake!
Amos5:20 The day of the Lord will bring darkness and not light; it will be a day of gloom

Amos6:2 Go and look at the city of Calneh. Then go on to the great city of Hamath and on down to the Philistine city of Gath. Were they any better than the kingdoms of Judah and Israel? Was their territory larger than yours?
Amos6:3 You refuse to admit that a day of disaster is coming
Amos6:4 How terrible it will be for you that stretch out on your luxurious couches
Amos6:5 You like to compose songs
Amos6:6 You drink wine by the bowlful and use the finest perfumes
Amos6:7 So you will be the first to go into exile. Your feasts and banquets will come to an end.
Amos6:8 The Sovereign Lord Almighty has given this solemn warning: “I hate the pride of the people of Israel; I despise their luxurious mansions. I will give their capital city and everything in it to the enemy.”
Amos6:9 If there are ten men left in a family
Amos6:10 The dead man's relative
Amos6:11 When the Lord gives the command
Amos6:12 Do horses gallop on rocks? Does anyone plow the sea with oxen? Yet you have turned justice into poison
Amos6:13 You brag about capturing the town of Lodebar. You boast
Amos6:14 The Lord God Almighty himself says
Amos7:1 I had a vision from the Sovereign Lord. In it I saw him create a swarm of locusts just after the king's share of the hay had been cut and the grass was starting to grow again.
Amos7:2 In my vision I saw the locusts eat up every green thing in the land
Amos7:3 The Lord changed his mind and said
Amos7:4 I had another vision from the Sovereign Lord. In it I saw him preparing to punish his people with fire. The fire burned up the great ocean under the earth and started to burn up the land.
Amos7:5 Then I said
Amos7:6 The Lord changed his mind again and said
Amos7:7 I had another vision from the Lord. In it I saw him standing beside a wall that had been built with the use of a plumb line
Amos7:8 He asked me
Amos7:9 The places where Isaac's descendants worship will be destroyed. The holy places of Israel will be left in ruins. I will bring the dynasty of King Jeroboam to an end.”

Obadiah1:1 This is the prophecy of Obadiah—what the Sovereign Lord said about the nation of Edom. The Lord has sent his messenger to the nations
Obadiah1:2 The Lord says to Edom
Obadiah1:3 Your pride has deceived you. Your capital is a fortress of solid rock; your home is high in the mountains
Obadiah1:4 Even though you make your home as high as an eagle's nest
Obadiah1:5 “When thieves come at night
Obadiah1:6 Descendants of Esau
Obadiah1:7 Your allies have deceived you; they have driven you from your country. People who were at peace with you have now conquered you. Those friends who ate with you have laid a trap for you; they say of you
Obadiah1:8 “On the day I punish Edom
Obadiah1:9 The fighting men of Teman will be terrified
Obadiah1:10 “Because you robbed and killed your relatives
Obadiah1:11 You stood aside on that day when enemies broke down their gates. You were as bad as those strangers who carried off Jerusalem's wealth and divided it among themselves.
Obadiah1:12 You should not have gloated over the misfortune of your relatives in Judah. You should not have been glad on the day of their ruin. You should not have laughed at them in their distress.
Obadiah1:13 You should not have entered the city of my people to gloat over their suffering and to seize their riches on the day of their disaster.
Obadiah1:14 You should not have stood at the crossroads to catch those trying to escape. You should not have handed them over to the enemy on the day of their distress.
Obadiah1:15 “The day is near when I
Obadiah1:16 My people have drunk a bitter cup of punishment on my sacred hill. But all the surrounding nations will drink a still more bitter cup of punishment; they will drink it all and vanish away.
Obadiah1:17 “But on Mount Zion some will escape
Obadiah1:18 The people of Jacob and of Joseph will be like fire; they will destroy the people of Esau as fire burns stubble. No descendant of Esau will survive. I
Obadiah1:19 “People from southern Judah will occupy Edom; those from the western foothills will capture Philistia. Israelites will possess the territory of Ephraim and Samaria; the people of Benjamin will take Gilead.
Obadiah1:20 The army of exiles from northern Israel will return and conquer Phoenicia as far north as Zarephath. The exiles from Jerusalem who are in Sardis will capture the towns of southern Judah.
Obadiah1:21 The victorious men of Jerusalem will attack Edom and rule over it. And the Lord himself will be king.”

Jonah1:2 He said
Jonah1:3 Jonah
Jonah1:4 But the Lord sent a strong wind on the sea
Jonah1:5 The sailors were terrified and cried out for help
Jonah1:6 The captain found him there and said to him
Jonah1:7 The sailors said to each other
Jonah1:8 So they said to him
Jonah1:9 “I am a Hebrew
Jonah1:10 Jonah went on to tell them that he was running away from the Lord. The sailors were terrified
Jonah1:11 The storm was getting worse all the time
Jonah1:12 Jonah answered
Jonah1:13 Instead
Jonah1:14 So they cried out to the Lord
Jonah1:15 Then they picked Jonah up and threw him into the sea
Jonah1:16 This made the sailors so afraid of the Lord that they offered a sacrifice and promised to serve him.

Jonah2:2 “In my distress
Jonah2:3 You threw me down into the depths
Jonah2:4 I thought I had been banished from your presence and would never see your holy Temple again.
Jonah2:5 The water came over me and choked me; the sea covered me completely
Jonah2:6 I went down to the very roots of the mountains
Jonah2:7 When I felt my life slipping away
Jonah2:8 Those who worship worthless idols have abandoned their loyalty to you.
Jonah2:9 But I will sing praises to you; I will offer you a sacrifice and do what I have promised. Salvation comes from the Lord!”
Jonah2:10 Then the Lord ordered the fish to spit Jonah up on the beach

Jonah3:2 He said
Jonah3:3 So Jonah obeyed the Lord and went to Nineveh

Jonah3:5 The people of Nineveh believed God's message. So they decided that everyone should fast
Jonah3:6 When the king of Nineveh heard about it
Jonah3:7 He sent out a proclamation to the people of Nineveh: “This is an order from the king and his officials: No one is to eat anything; all persons
Jonah3:8 All persons and animals must wear sackcloth. Everyone must pray earnestly to God and must give up their wicked behavior and their evil actions.
Jonah3:9 Perhaps God will change his mind; perhaps he will stop being angry
Jonah3:10 God saw what they did; he saw that they had given up their wicked behavior. So he changed his mind and did not punish them as he had said he would.

Micah1:2 Hear this

Micah2:2 When they want fields

Micah2:4 When that time comes
Micah2:5 So then

Micah2:9 You drive the women of my people out of the homes they love

Micah3:1 Listen
Micah3:2 yet you hate what is good and you love what is evil. You skin my people alive and tear the flesh off their bones.
Micah3:3 You eat my people up. You strip off their skin
Micah3:4 The time is coming when you will cry out to the Lord
Micah3:5 My people are deceived by prophets who promise peace to those who pay them

Micah3:7 Those who predict the future will be disgraced by their failure. They will all be humiliated because God does not answer them.

Habakkuk1:10 They treat kings with contempt and laugh at high officials. No fortress can stop them—they pile up earth against it and capture it.
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep on like the wind and are gone
Habakkuk1:12 Lord

Habakkuk2:4 And this is the message: ‘Those who are evil will not survive

Habakkuk2:7 But before you know it

Habakkuk2:9 You are doomed! You have made your family rich with what you took by violence
Habakkuk2:10 But your schemes have brought shame on your family; by destroying many nations you have only brought ruin on yourself.
Habakkuk2:11 Even the stones of the walls cry out against you
Habakkuk2:12 You are doomed! You founded a city on crime and built it up by murder.
Habakkuk2:13 The nations you conquered wore themselves out in useless labor
Habakkuk2:14 But the earth will be as full of the knowledge of the Lord 's glory as the seas are full of water.
Habakkuk2:15 You are doomed! In your fury you humiliated and disgraced your neighbors; you made them stagger as though they were drunk.
Habakkuk2:16 You in turn will be covered with shame instead of honor. You yourself will drink and stagger. The Lord will make you drink your own cup of punishment
Habakkuk2:17 You have cut down the forests of Lebanon; now you will be cut down. You killed its animals; now animals will terrify you. This will happen because of the murders you have committed and because of your violence against the people of the world and its cities.
Habakkuk2:18 What's the use of an idol? It is only something that a human being has made
Habakkuk2:19 You are doomed! You say to a piece of wood
Habakkuk2:20 The Lord is in his holy Temple; let everyone on earth be silent in his presence.

Matthew10:6 Instead

Matthew10:8 Heal the sick
Matthew10:9 Do not carry any gold
Matthew10:10 do not carry a beggar's bag for the trip or an extra shirt or shoes or a walking stick. Workers should be given what they need.
Matthew10:11 “When you come to a town or village
Matthew10:12 When you go into a house
Matthew10:13 If the people in that house welcome you
Matthew10:14 And if some home or town will not welcome you or listen to you
Matthew10:15 I assure you that on the Judgment Day God will show more mercy to the people of Sodom and Gomorrah than to the people of that town!
Matthew10:16 “Listen! I am sending you out just like sheep to a pack of wolves. You must be as cautious as snakes and as gentle as doves.
Matthew10:17 Watch out
Matthew10:18 For my sake you will be brought to trial before rulers and kings
Matthew10:19 When they bring you to trial
Matthew10:20 For the words you will speak will not be yours; they will come from the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.
Matthew10:21 “People will hand over their own brothers to be put to death
Matthew10:22 Everyone will hate you because of me. But whoever holds out to the end will be saved.
Matthew10:23 When they persecute you in one town
Matthew10:24 “No pupil is greater than his teacher; no slave is greater than his master.
Matthew10:25 So a pupil should be satisfied to become like his teacher
Matthew10:26 “So do not be afraid of people. Whatever is now covered up will be uncovered
Matthew10:27 What I am telling you in the dark you must repeat in broad daylight
Matthew10:28 Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul; rather be afraid of God
Matthew10:29 For only a penny you can buy two sparrows
Matthew10:30 As for you
Matthew10:31 So do not be afraid; you are worth much more than many sparrows!
Matthew10:32 “Those who declare publicly that they belong to me
Matthew10:33 But those who reject me publicly
Matthew10:34 “Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the world. No
Matthew10:35 I came to set sons against their fathers
Matthew10:36 your worst enemies will be the members of your own family.
Matthew10:37 “Those who love their father or mother more than me are not fit to be my disciples; those who love their son or daughter more than me are not fit to be my disciples.
Matthew10:38 Those who do not take up their cross and follow in my steps are not fit to be my disciples.
Matthew10:39 Those who try to gain their own life will lose it; but those who lose their life for my sake will gain it.
Matthew10:40 “Whoever welcomes you welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me.
Matthew10:41 Whoever welcomes God's messenger because he is God's messenger
Matthew10:42 You can be sure that whoever gives even a drink of cold water to one of the least of these my followers because he is my follower

Matthew13:1 That same day Jesus left the house and went to the lakeside
Matthew13:2 The crowd that gathered around him was so large that he got into a boat and sat in it
Matthew13:3 He used parables to tell them many things. “Once there was a man who went out to sow grain.

Matthew13:5 Some of it fell on rocky ground
Matthew13:6 But when the sun came up
Matthew13:7 Some of the seed fell among thorn bushes
Matthew13:8 But some seeds fell in good soil
Matthew13:9 And Jesus concluded
Matthew13:10 Then the disciples came to Jesus and asked him
Matthew13:11 Jesus answered
Matthew13:12 For the person who has something will be given more
Matthew13:13 The reason I use parables in talking to them is that they look
Matthew13:14 So the prophecy of Isaiah applies to them: ‘This people will listen and listen
Matthew13:15 because their minds are dull
Matthew13:16 “As for you

Matthew13:18 “Listen
Matthew13:19 Those who hear the message about the Kingdom but do not understand it are like the seeds that fell along the path. The Evil One comes and snatches away what was sown in them.
Matthew13:20 The seeds that fell on rocky ground stand for those who receive the message gladly as soon as they hear it.
Matthew13:21 But it does not sink deep into them
Matthew13:22 The seeds that fell among thorn bushes stand for those who hear the message; but the worries about this life and the love for riches choke the message
Matthew13:23 And the seeds sown in the good soil stand for those who hear the message and understand it: they bear fruit
Matthew13:24 Jesus told them another parable: “The Kingdom of heaven is like this. A man sowed good seed in his field.
Matthew13:25 One night
Matthew13:26 When the plants grew and the heads of grain began to form
Matthew13:27 The man's servants came to him and said
Matthew13:28 ‘It was some enemy who did this
Matthew13:29 ‘No
Matthew13:30 Let the wheat and the weeds both grow together until harvest. Then I will tell the harvest workers to pull up the weeds first
Matthew13:31 Jesus told them another parable: “The Kingdom of heaven is like this. A man takes a mustard seed and sows it in his field.
Matthew13:32 It is the smallest of all seeds
Matthew13:33 Jesus told them still another parable: “The Kingdom of heaven is like this. A woman takes some yeast and mixes it with a bushel of flour until the whole batch of dough rises.”
Matthew13:34 Jesus used parables to tell all these things to the crowds; he would not say a thing to them without using a parable.
Matthew13:35 He did this to make come true what the prophet had said
Matthew13:36 When Jesus had left the crowd and gone indoors
Matthew13:37 Jesus answered
Matthew13:38 the field is the world; the good seed is the people who belong to the Kingdom; the weeds are the people who belong to the Evil One;
Matthew13:39 and the enemy who sowed the weeds is the Devil. The harvest is the end of the age
Matthew13:40 Just as the weeds are gathered up and burned in the fire

Matthew13:42 and they will throw them into the fiery furnace
Matthew13:43 Then God's people will shine like the sun in their Father's Kingdom. Listen
Matthew13:44 “The Kingdom of heaven is like this. A man happens to find a treasure hidden in a field. He covers it up again
Matthew13:45 “Also
Matthew13:46 and when he finds one that is unusually fine
Matthew13:47 “Also
Matthew13:48 When the net is full
Matthew13:49 It will be like this at the end of the age: the angels will go out and gather up the evil people from among the good
Matthew13:50 and will throw them into the fiery furnace

Matthew13:52 So he replied

Matthew13:56 Aren't all his sisters living here? Where did he get all this?”

Mark4:29 When the grain is ripe

Mark4:31 It is like this. A man takes a mustard seed
Mark4:32 After a while it grows up and becomes the biggest of all plants. It puts out such large branches that the birds come and make their nests in its shade.”

Luke8:5 “Once there was a man who went out to sow grain. As he scattered the seed in the field
Luke8:6 Some of it fell on rocky ground
Luke8:7 Some of the seed fell among thorn bushes
Luke8:8 And some seeds fell in good soil; the plants grew and bore grain
Luke8:9 His disciples asked Jesus what this parable meant
Luke8:10 and he answered
Luke8:11 “This is what the parable means: the seed is the word of God.
Luke8:12 The seeds that fell along the path stand for those who hear; but the Devil comes and takes the message away from their hearts in order to keep them from believing and being saved.
Luke8:13 The seeds that fell on rocky ground stand for those who hear the message and receive it gladly. But it does not sink deep into them; they believe only for a while but when the time of testing comes
Luke8:14 The seeds that fell among thorn bushes stand for those who hear; but the worries and riches and pleasures of this life crowd in and choke them
Luke8:15 The seeds that fell in good soil stand for those who hear the message and retain it in a good and obedient heart
Luke8:16 “No one lights a lamp and covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead

Luke12:25 Can any of you live a bit longer by worrying about it?
Luke12:26 If you can't manage even such a small thing
Luke12:27 Look how the wild flowers grow: they don't work or make clothes for themselves. But I tell you that not even King Solomon with all his wealth had clothes as beautiful as one of these flowers.
Luke12:28 It is God who clothes the wild grass—grass that is here today and gone tomorrow
Luke12:29 “So don't be all upset
Luke12:30 (For the pagans of this world are always concerned about all these things.) Your Father knows that you need these things.
Luke12:31 Instead

Luke12:42 The Lord answered

Luke12:44 Indeed

Luke12:46 then the master will come back one day when the servant does not expect him and at a time he does not know. The master will cut him in pieces and make him share the fate of the disobedient.

Acts20:5 They went ahead and waited for us in Troas.

Acts20:7 On Saturday evening we gathered together for the fellowship meal. Paul spoke to the people and kept on speaking until midnight

Acts20:10 But Paul went down and threw himself on him and hugged him. “Don't worry
Acts20:11 Then he went back upstairs
Acts20:12 They took the young man home alive and were greatly comforted.

Acts20:14 When he met us in Assos

Acts20:17 From Miletus Paul sent a message to Ephesus
Acts20:18 When they arrived
Acts20:19 With all humility and many tears I did my work as the Lord's servant during the hard times that came to me because of the plots of some Jews.
Acts20:20 You know that I did not hold back anything that would be of help to you as I preached and taught in public and in your homes.
Acts20:21 To Jews and Gentiles alike I gave solemn warning that they should turn from their sins to God and believe in our Lord Jesus.
Acts20:22 And now
Acts20:23 I only know that in every city the Holy Spirit has warned me that prison and troubles wait for me.
Acts20:24 But I reckon my own life to be worth nothing to me; I only want to complete my mission and finish the work that the Lord Jesus gave me to do
Acts20:25 “I have gone about among all of you
Acts20:26 So I solemnly declare to you this very day: if any of you should be lost
Acts20:27 For I have not held back from announcing to you the whole purpose of God.
Acts20:28 So keep watch over yourselves and over all the flock which the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be shepherds of the church of God
Acts20:29 I know that after I leave
Acts20:30 The time will come when some men from your own group will tell lies to lead the believers away after them.
Acts20:31 Watch
Acts20:32 “And now I commend you to the care of God and to the message of his grace
Acts20:33 I have not wanted anyone's silver or gold or clothing.
Acts20:34 You yourselves know that I have worked with these hands of mine to provide everything that my companions and I have needed.
Acts20:35 I have shown you in all things that by working hard in this way we must help the weak
Acts20:36 When Paul finished
Acts20:37 They were all crying as they hugged him and kissed him good-bye.
Acts20:38 They were especially sad because he had said that they would never see him again. And so they went with him to the ship.

Acts27:8 We kept close to the coast and with great difficulty came to a place called Safe Harbors
Acts27:9 We spent a long time there
Acts27:10 “Men
Acts27:11 But the army officer was convinced by what the captain and the owner of the ship said
Acts27:12 The harbor was not a good one to spend the winter in; so almost everyone was in favor of putting out to sea and trying to reach Phoenix
Acts27:13 A soft wind from the south began to blow
Acts27:14 But soon a very strong wind—the one called “Northeaster”—blew down from the island.
Acts27:15 It hit the ship
Acts27:16 We got some shelter when we passed to the south of the little island of Cauda. There
Acts27:17 They pulled it aboard and then fastened some ropes tight around the ship. They were afraid that they might run into the sandbanks off the coast of Libya

Acts27:19 and on the following day they threw part of the ship's equipment overboard.

Acts27:24 and said
Acts27:25 So take courage
Acts27:26 But we will be driven ashore on some island.”
Acts27:27 It was the fourteenth night

Acts27:29 They were afraid that the ship would go on the rocks

Acts28:4 The natives saw the snake hanging on Paul's hand and said to one another
Acts28:5 But Paul shook the snake off into the fire without being harmed at all.

Romans1:1 From Paul
Romans1:2 The Good News was promised long ago by God through his prophets
Romans1:3 It is about his Son
Romans1:4 as to his divine holiness
Romans1:5 Through him God gave me the privilege of being an apostle for the sake of Christ
Romans1:6 This also includes you who are in Rome
Romans1:7 And so I write to all of you in Rome whom God loves and has called to be his own people: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 God is my witness that what I say is true—the God whom I serve with all my heart by preaching the Good News about his Son. God knows that I remember you
Romans1:10 every time I pray. I ask that God in his good will may at last make it possible for me to visit you now.
Romans1:11 For I want very much to see you
Romans1:12 What I mean is that both you and I will be helped at the same time
Romans1:13 You must remember
Romans1:14 For I have an obligation to all peoples
Romans1:15 So then
Romans1:16 I have complete confidence in the gospel; it is God's power to save all who believe
Romans1:17 For the gospel reveals how God puts people right with himself: it is through faith from beginning to end. As the scripture says
Romans1:18 God's anger is revealed from heaven against all the sin and evil of the people whose evil ways prevent the truth from being known.
Romans1:19 God punishes them
Romans1:20 Ever since God created the world
Romans1:21 They know God
Romans1:22 They say they are wise
Romans1:23 instead of worshiping the immortal God
Romans1:24 And so God has given those people over to do the filthy things their hearts desire
Romans1:25 They exchange the truth about God for a lie; they worship and serve what God has created instead of the Creator himself
Romans1:26 Because they do this
Romans1:27 In the same way the men give up natural sexual relations with women and burn with passion for each other. Men do shameful things with each other
Romans1:28 Because those people refuse to keep in mind the true knowledge about God
Romans1:29 They are filled with all kinds of wickedness
Romans1:30 and speak evil of one another; they are hateful to God
Romans1:31 they have no conscience; they do not keep their promises
Romans1:32 They know that God's law says that people who live in this way deserve death. Yet
Romans2:1 Do you
Romans2:2 We know that God is right when he judges the people who do such things as these.
Romans2:3 But you
Romans2:4 Or perhaps you despise his great kindness
Romans2:5 But you have a hard and stubborn heart
Romans2:6 For God will reward each of us according to what we have done.
Romans2:7 Some people keep on doing good
Romans2:8 Other people are selfish and reject what is right
Romans2:9 There will be suffering and pain for all those who do what is evil
Romans2:10 But God will give glory
Romans2:11 For God judges everyone by the same standard.
Romans2:12 The Gentiles do not have the Law of Moses; they sin and are lost apart from the Law. The Jews have the Law; they sin and are judged by the Law.
Romans2:13 For it is not by hearing the Law that people are put right with God
Romans2:14 The Gentiles do not have the Law; but whenever they do by instinct what the Law commands
Romans2:15 Their conduct shows that what the Law commands is written in their hearts. Their consciences also show that this is true
Romans2:16 And so
Romans2:17 What about you? You call yourself a Jew; you depend on the Law and boast about God;
Romans2:18 you know what God wants you to do
Romans2:19 you are sure that you are a guide for the blind
Romans2:20 an instructor for the foolish
Romans2:21 You teach others—why don't you teach yourself? You preach
Romans2:22 You say
Romans2:23 You boast about having God's law—but do you bring shame on God by breaking his law?
Romans2:24 The scripture says
Romans2:25 If you obey the Law
Romans2:26 If the Gentile
Romans2:27 And so you Jews will be condemned by the Gentiles because you break the Law
Romans2:28 After all
Romans2:29 Rather
Romans3:1 Do the Jews then have any advantage over the Gentiles? Or is there any value in being circumcised?
Romans3:2 Much
Romans3:3 But what if some of them were not faithful? Does this mean that God will not be faithful?
Romans3:4 Certainly not! God must be true
Romans3:5 But what if our doing wrong serves to show up more clearly God's doing right? Can we say that God does wrong when he punishes us? (This would be the natural question to ask.)
Romans3:6 By no means! If God is not just
Romans3:7 But what if my untruth serves God's glory by making his truth stand out more clearly? Why should I still be condemned as a sinner?
Romans3:8 Why not say
Romans3:9 Well then
Romans3:10 As the Scriptures say: “There is no one who is righteous
Romans3:11 no one who is wise or who worships God.
Romans3:12 All have turned away from God; they have all gone wrong; no one does what is right
Romans3:13 Their words are full of deadly deceit; wicked lies roll off their tongues
Romans3:14 their speech is filled with bitter curses.
Romans3:15 They are quick to hurt and kill;
Romans3:16 they leave ruin and destruction wherever they go.
Romans3:17 They have not known the path of peace
Romans3:18 nor have they learned reverence for God.”
Romans3:19 Now we know that everything in the Law applies to those who live under the Law

Galatians5:4 Those of you who try to be put right with God by obeying the Law have cut yourselves off from Christ. You are outside God's grace.
Galatians5:5 As for us
Galatians5:6 For when we are in union with Christ Jesus
Galatians5:7 You were doing so well! Who made you stop obeying the truth? How did he persuade you?
Galatians5:8 It was not done by God
Galatians5:9 “It takes only a little yeast to make the whole batch of dough rise
Galatians5:10 But I still feel confident about you. Our life in union with the Lord makes me confident that you will not take a different view and that whoever is upsetting you will be punished by God.
Galatians5:11 But as for me
Galatians5:12 I wish that the people who are upsetting you would go all the way; let them go on and castrate themselves!
Galatians5:13 As for you
Galatians5:14 For the whole Law is summed up in one commandment: “Love your neighbor as you love yourself.”
Galatians5:15 But if you act like wild animals
Galatians5:16 What I say is this: let the Spirit direct your lives
Galatians5:17 For what our human nature wants is opposed to what the Spirit wants
Galatians5:18 If the Spirit leads you
Galatians5:19 What human nature does is quite plain. It shows itself in immoral
Galatians5:20 in worship of idols and witchcraft. People become enemies and they fight; they become jealous
Galatians5:21 they are envious
Galatians5:22 But the Spirit produces love
Galatians5:23 humility
Galatians5:24 And those who belong to Christ Jesus have put to death their human nature with all its passions and desires.
Galatians5:25 The Spirit has given us life; he must also control our lives.
Galatians5:26 We must not be proud or irritate one another or be jealous of one another.
Galatians6:1 My friends
Galatians6:2 Help carry one another's burdens
Galatians6:3 If you think you are something when you really are nothing
Galatians6:4 You should each judge your own conduct. If it is good
Galatians6:5 For each of you have to carry your own load.
Galatians6:6 If you are being taught the Christian message
Galatians6:7 Do not deceive yourselves; no one makes a fool of God. You will reap exactly what you plant.
Galatians6:8 If you plant in the field of your natural desires
Galatians6:9 So let us not become tired of doing good; for if we do not give up
Galatians6:10 So then
Galatians6:11 See what big letters I make as I write to you now with my own hand!

Galatians6:13 Even those who practice circumcision do not obey the Law; they want you to be circumcised so that they can boast that you submitted to this physical ceremony.
Galatians6:14 As for me
Galatians6:15 It does not matter at all whether or not one is circumcised; what does matter is being a new creature.
Galatians6:16 As for those who follow this rule in their lives
Galatians6:17 To conclude: let no one give me any more trouble

Philippians1:21 For what is life? To me
Philippians1:22 But if by continuing to live I can do more worthwhile work
Philippians1:23 I am pulled in two directions. I want very much to leave this life and be with Christ

Colossians4:18 With my own hand I write this:. Do not forget my chains! May God's grace be with you.
1Thessalonians1:1 From Paul
1Thessalonians1:2 We always thank God for you all and always mention you in our prayers.
1Thessalonians1:3 For we remember before our God and Father how you put your faith into practice
1Thessalonians1:4 Our friends
1Thessalonians1:5 For we brought the Good News to you
1Thessalonians1:6 You imitated us and the Lord; and even though you suffered much
1Thessalonians1:7 So you became an example to all believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 For not only did the message about the Lord go out from you throughout Macedonia and Achaia
1Thessalonians1:9 All those people speak about how you received us when we visited you
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for his Son to come from heaven—his Son Jesus
1Thessalonians2:1 Our friends
1Thessalonians2:2 You know how we had already been mistreated and insulted in Philippi before we came to you in Thessalonica. And even though there was much opposition
1Thessalonians2:3 Our appeal to you is not based on error or impure motives
1Thessalonians2:4 Instead
1Thessalonians2:5 You know very well that we did not come to you with flattering talk
1Thessalonians2:6 We did not try to get praise from anyone
1Thessalonians2:7 even though as apostles of Christ we could have made demands on you. But we were gentle when we were with you
1Thessalonians2:8 Because of our love for you we were ready to share with you not only the Good News from God but even our own lives. You were so dear to us!
1Thessalonians2:9 Surely you remember
1Thessalonians2:10 You are our witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 You know that we treated each one of you just as parents treat their own children.
1Thessalonians2:12 We encouraged you
1Thessalonians2:13 And there is another reason why we always give thanks to God. When we brought you God's message
1Thessalonians2:14 Our friends
1Thessalonians2:15 who killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets
1Thessalonians2:16 They even tried to stop us from preaching to the Gentiles the message that would bring them salvation. In this way they have brought to completion all the sins they have always committed. And now God's anger has at last come down on them!
1Thessalonians2:17 As for us
1Thessalonians2:18 We wanted to return to you. I myself tried to go back more than once
1Thessalonians2:19 After all
1Thessalonians2:20 Indeed
1Thessalonians3:1 Finally
1Thessalonians3:2 while we sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 so that none of you should turn back because of these persecutions. You yourselves know that such persecutions are part of God's will for us.
1Thessalonians3:4 For while we were still with you
1Thessalonians3:5 That is why I had to send Timothy. I could not bear it any longer
1Thessalonians3:6 Now Timothy has come back
1Thessalonians3:7 So
1Thessalonians3:8 because now we really live if you stand firm in your life in union with the Lord.
1Thessalonians3:9 Now we can give thanks to our God for you. We thank him for the joy we have in his presence because of you.
1Thessalonians3:10 Day and night we ask him with all our heart to let us see you personally and supply what is needed in your faith.
1Thessalonians3:11 May our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus prepare the way for us to come to you!
1Thessalonians3:12 May the Lord make your love for one another and for all people grow more and more and become as great as our love for you.
1Thessalonians3:13 In this way he will strengthen you
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally
1Thessalonians4:2 For you know the instructions we gave you by the authority of the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 God wants you to be holy and completely free from sexual immorality.
1Thessalonians4:4 Each of you should know how to live with your wife in a holy and honorable way
1Thessalonians4:5 not with a lustful desire
1Thessalonians4:6 In this matter
1Thessalonians4:7 God did not call us to live in immorality
1Thessalonians4:8 So then
1Thessalonians4:9 There is no need to write you about love for each other. You yourselves have been taught by God how you should love one another.
1Thessalonians4:10 And you have
1Thessalonians4:11 Make it your aim to live a quiet life
1Thessalonians4:12 In this way you will win the respect of those who are not believers
1Thessalonians4:13 Our friends
1Thessalonians4:14 We believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 What we are teaching you now is the Lord's teaching: we who are alive on the day the Lord comes will not go ahead of those who have died.
1Thessalonians4:16 There will be the shout of command
1Thessalonians4:17 then we who are living at that time will be gathered up along with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And so we will always be with the Lord.
1Thessalonians4:18 So then
1Thessalonians5:1 There is no need to write you
1Thessalonians5:2 For you yourselves know very well that the Day of the Lord will come as a thief comes at night.
1Thessalonians5:3 When people say
1Thessalonians5:4 But you
1Thessalonians5:5 All of you are people who belong to the light
1Thessalonians5:6 So then
1Thessalonians5:7 It is at night when people sleep; it is at night when they get drunk.
1Thessalonians5:8 But we belong to the day
1Thessalonians5:9 God did not choose us to suffer his anger
1Thessalonians5:10 who died for us in order that we might live together with him
1Thessalonians5:11 And so encourage one another and help one another
1Thessalonians5:12 We beg you
1Thessalonians5:13 Treat them with the greatest respect and love because of the work they do. Be at peace among yourselves.
1Thessalonians5:14 We urge you
1Thessalonians5:15 See that no one pays back wrong for wrong
1Thessalonians5:16 Be joyful always
1Thessalonians5:17 pray at all times
1Thessalonians5:18 be thankful in all circumstances. This is what God wants from you in your life in union with Christ Jesus.
1Thessalonians5:19 Do not restrain the Holy Spirit;
1Thessalonians5:20 do not despise inspired messages.
1Thessalonians5:21 Put all things to the test: keep what is good
1Thessalonians5:22 and avoid every kind of evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 May the God who gives us peace make you holy in every way and keep your whole being—spirit
1Thessalonians5:24 He who calls you will do it
1Thessalonians5:25 Pray also for us
1Thessalonians5:26 Greet all the believers with the kiss of peace.
1Thessalonians5:27 I urge you by the authority of the Lord to read this letter to all the believers.
1Thessalonians5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
2Thessalonians1:1 From Paul
2Thessalonians1:2 May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
2Thessalonians1:3 Our friends
2Thessalonians1:4 That is why we ourselves boast about you in the churches of God. We boast about the way you continue to endure and believe through all the persecutions and sufferings you are experiencing.
2Thessalonians1:5 All of this proves that God's judgment is just and as a result you will become worthy of his Kingdom
2Thessalonians1:6 God will do what is right: he will bring suffering on those who make you suffer
2Thessalonians1:7 and he will give relief to you who suffer and to us as well. He will do this when the Lord Jesus appears from heaven with his mighty angels
2Thessalonians1:8 with a flaming fire
2Thessalonians1:9 They will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction
2Thessalonians1:10 when he comes on that Day to receive glory from all his people and honor from all who believe. You too will be among them
2Thessalonians1:11 That is why we always pray for you. We ask our God to make you worthy of the life he has called you to live. May he fulfill by his power all your desire for goodness and complete your work of faith.
2Thessalonians1:12 In this way the name of our Lord Jesus will receive glory from you
2Thessalonians2:1 Concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to be with him: I beg you
2Thessalonians2:2 not to be so easily confused in your thinking or upset by the claim that the Day of the Lord has come. Perhaps it is thought that we said this while prophesying or preaching
2Thessalonians2:3 Do not let anyone deceive you in any way. For the Day will not come until the final Rebellion takes place and the Wicked One appears
2Thessalonians2:4 He will oppose every so-called god or object of worship and will put himself above them all. He will even go in and sit down in God's Temple and claim to be God.
2Thessalonians2:5 Don't you remember? I told you all this while I was with you.
2Thessalonians2:6 Yet there is something that keeps this from happening now
2Thessalonians2:7 The Mysterious Wickedness is already at work
2Thessalonians2:8 Then the Wicked One will be revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 The Wicked One will come with the power of Satan and perform all kinds of false miracles and wonders
2Thessalonians2:10 and use every kind of wicked deceit on those who will perish. They will perish because they did not welcome and love the truth so as to be saved.
2Thessalonians2:11 And so God sends the power of error to work in them so that they believe what is false.
2Thessalonians2:12 The result is that all who have not believed the truth
2Thessalonians2:13 We must thank God at all times for you
2Thessalonians2:14 God called you to this through the Good News we preached to you; he called you to possess your share of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians2:15 So then
2Thessalonians2:16 May our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father
2Thessalonians2:17 encourage you and strengthen you to always do and say what is good.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 Pray also that God will rescue us from wicked and evil people; for not everyone believes the message.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful
2Thessalonians3:4 And the Lord gives us confidence in you
2Thessalonians3:5 May the Lord lead you into a greater understanding of God's love and the endurance that is given by Christ.
2Thessalonians3:6 Our friends
2Thessalonians3:7 You yourselves know very well that you should do just what we did. We were not lazy when we were with you.
2Thessalonians3:8 We did not accept anyone's support without paying for it. Instead
2Thessalonians3:9 We did this
2Thessalonians3:10 While we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 We say this because we hear that there are some people among you who live lazy lives and who do nothing except meddle in other people's business.
2Thessalonians3:12 In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ we command these people and warn them to lead orderly lives and work to earn their own living.
2Thessalonians3:13 But you
2Thessalonians3:14 It may be that some there will not obey the message we send you in this letter. If so
2Thessalonians3:15 But do not treat them as enemies; instead
2Thessalonians3:16 May the Lord himself
2Thessalonians3:17 With my own hand I write this:. This is the way I sign every letter; this is how I write.
2Thessalonians3:18 May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1Timothy1:1 From Paul
1Timothy1:2 To Timothy
1Timothy1:3 I want you to stay in Ephesus
1Timothy1:4 Tell them to give up those legends and those long lists of ancestors
1Timothy1:5 The purpose of this order is to arouse the love that comes from a pure heart
1Timothy1:6 Some people have turned away from these and have lost their way in foolish discussions.
1Timothy1:7 They want to be teachers of God's law
1Timothy1:8 We know that the Law is good if it is used as it should be used.
1Timothy1:9 It must be remembered
1Timothy1:10 for the immoral
1Timothy1:11 That teaching is found in the gospel that was entrusted to me to announce
1Timothy1:12 I give thanks to Christ Jesus our Lord
1Timothy1:13 even though in the past I spoke evil of him and persecuted and insulted him. But God was merciful to me because I did not yet have faith and so did not know what I was doing.
1Timothy1:14 And our Lord poured out his abundant grace on me and gave me the faith and love which are ours in union with Christ Jesus.
1Timothy1:15 This is a true saying
1Timothy1:16 but God was merciful to me in order that Christ Jesus might show his full patience in dealing with me
1Timothy1:17 To the eternal King
1Timothy1:18 Timothy
1Timothy1:19 and keep your faith and a clear conscience. Some people have not listened to their conscience and have made a ruin of their faith.
1Timothy1:20 Among them are Hymenaeus and Alexander
1Timothy2:1 First of all
1Timothy2:2 for kings and all others who are in authority
1Timothy2:3 This is good and it pleases God our Savior
1Timothy2:4 who wants everyone to be saved and to come to know the truth.
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God
1Timothy2:6 who gave himself to redeem the whole human race. That was the proof at the right time that God wants everyone to be saved
1Timothy2:7 and that is why I was sent as an apostle and teacher of the Gentiles
1Timothy2:8 In every church service I want the men to pray
1Timothy2:9 I also want the women to be modest and sensible about their clothes and to dress properly; not with fancy hair styles or with gold ornaments or pearls or expensive dresses
1Timothy2:10 but with good deeds
1Timothy2:11 Women should learn in silence and all humility.
1Timothy2:12 I do not allow them to teach or to have authority over men; they must keep quiet.
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was created first
1Timothy2:14 And it was not Adam who was deceived; it was the woman who was deceived and broke God's law.
1Timothy2:15 But a woman will be saved through having children
1Timothy3:1 This is a true saying: If a man is eager to be a church leader
1Timothy3:2 A church leader must be without fault; he must have only one wife
1Timothy3:3 he must not be a drunkard or a violent man
1Timothy3:4 he must be able to manage his own family well and make his children obey him with all respect.
1Timothy3:5 For if a man does not know how to manage his own family
1Timothy3:6 He must be mature in the faith
1Timothy3:7 He should be a man who is respected by the people outside the church
1Timothy3:8 Church helpers must also have a good character and be sincere; they must not drink too much wine or be greedy for money;
1Timothy3:9 they should hold to the revealed truth of the faith with a clear conscience.
1Timothy3:10 They should be tested first
1Timothy3:11 Their wives also must be of good character and must not gossip; they must be sober and honest in everything.
1Timothy3:12 A church helper must have only one wife
1Timothy3:13 Those helpers who do their work well win for themselves a good standing and are able to speak boldly about their faith in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy3:14 As I write this letter to you
1Timothy3:15 But if I delay
1Timothy3:16 No one can deny how great is the secret of our religion: He appeared in human form
1Timothy4:1 The Spirit says clearly that some people will abandon the faith in later times; they will obey lying spirits and follow the teachings of demons.
1Timothy4:2 Such teachings are spread by deceitful liars
1Timothy4:3 Such people teach that it is wrong to marry and to eat certain foods. But God created those foods to be eaten
1Timothy4:4 Everything that God has created is good; nothing is to be rejected
1Timothy4:5 because the word of God and the prayer make it acceptable to God.
1Timothy4:6 If you give these instructions to the believers
1Timothy4:7 But keep away from those godless legends
1Timothy4:8 Physical exercise has some value
1Timothy4:9 This is a true saying
1Timothy4:10 We struggle and work hard
1Timothy4:11 Give them these instructions and these teachings.
1Timothy4:12 Do not let anyone look down on you because you are young
1Timothy4:13 Until I come
1Timothy4:14 Do not neglect the spiritual gift that is in you
1Timothy4:15 Practice these things and devote yourself to them
1Timothy4:16 Watch yourself and watch your teaching. Keep on doing these things
1Timothy5:1 Do not rebuke an older man
1Timothy5:2 the older women as mothers
1Timothy5:3 Show respect for widows who really are all alone.
1Timothy5:4 But if a widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 A widow who is all alone
1Timothy5:6 But a widow who gives herself to pleasure has already died
1Timothy5:7 Give them these instructions
1Timothy5:8 But if any do not take care of their relatives
1Timothy5:9 Do not add any widow to the list of widows unless she is over sixty years of age. In addition
1Timothy5:10 and have a reputation for good deeds: a woman who brought up her children well
1Timothy5:11 But do not include younger widows in the list; because when their desires make them want to marry
1Timothy5:12 and so become guilty of breaking their earlier promise to him.
1Timothy5:13 They also learn to waste their time in going around from house to house; but even worse
1Timothy5:14 So I would prefer that the younger widows get married
1Timothy5:15 For some widows have already turned away to follow Satan.
1Timothy5:16 But if any Christian woman has widows in her family
1Timothy5:17 The elders who do good work as leaders should be considered worthy of receiving double pay
1Timothy5:18 For the scripture says
1Timothy5:19 Do not listen to an accusation against an elder unless it is brought by two or more witnesses.
1Timothy5:20 Rebuke publicly all those who commit sins
1Timothy5:21 In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the holy angels I solemnly call upon you to obey these instructions without showing any prejudice or favor to anyone in anything you do.
1Timothy5:22 Be in no hurry to lay hands on people to dedicate them to the Lord's service. Take no part in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
1Timothy5:23 Do not drink water only
1Timothy5:24 The sins of some people are plain to see
1Timothy5:25 In the same way good deeds are plainly seen
1Timothy6:1 Those who are slaves must consider their masters worthy of all respect
1Timothy6:2 Slaves belonging to Christian masters must not despise them
1Timothy6:3 Whoever teaches a different doctrine and does not agree with the true words of our Lord Jesus Christ and with the teaching of our religion
1Timothy6:4 is swollen with pride and knows nothing. He has an unhealthy desire to argue and quarrel about words
1Timothy6:5 and constant arguments from people whose minds do not function and who no longer have the truth. They think that religion is a way to become rich.
1Timothy6:6 Well
1Timothy6:7 What did we bring into the world? Nothing! What can we take out of the world? Nothing!
1Timothy6:8 So then
1Timothy6:9 But those who want to get rich fall into temptation and are caught in the trap of many foolish and harmful desires
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is a source of all kinds of evil. Some have been so eager to have it that they have wandered away from the faith and have broken their hearts with many sorrows.
1Timothy6:11 But you
1Timothy6:12 Run your best in the race of faith
1Timothy6:13 Before God
1Timothy6:14 to obey your orders and keep them faithfully until the Day when our Lord Jesus Christ will appear.
1Timothy6:15 His appearing will be brought about at the right time by God
1Timothy6:16 He alone is immortal; he lives in the light that no one can approach. No one has ever seen him; no one can ever see him. To him be honor and eternal power! Amen.
1Timothy6:17 Command those who are rich in the things of this life not to be proud
1Timothy6:18 Command them to do good
1Timothy6:19 In this way they will store up for themselves a treasure which will be a solid foundation for the future. And then they will be able to win the life which is true life.
1Timothy6:20 Timothy
1Timothy6:21 For some have claimed to possess it
2Timothy1:1 From Paul
2Timothy1:2 To Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I give thanks to God
2Timothy1:4 I remember your tears
2Timothy1:5 I remember the sincere faith you have
2Timothy1:6 For this reason I remind you to keep alive the gift that God gave you when I laid my hands on you.
2Timothy1:7 For the Spirit that God has given us does not make us timid; instead
2Timothy1:8 Do not be ashamed
2Timothy1:9 He saved us and called us to be his own people
2Timothy1:10 but now it has been revealed to us through the coming of our Savior
2Timothy1:11 God has appointed me as an apostle and teacher to proclaim the Good News
2Timothy1:12 and it is for this reason that I suffer these things. But I am still full of confidence
2Timothy1:13 Hold firmly to the true words that I taught you
2Timothy1:14 Through the power of the Holy Spirit
2Timothy1:15 You know that everyone in the province of Asia
2Timothy1:16 May the Lord show mercy to the family of Onesiphorus
2Timothy1:17 but as soon as he arrived in Rome
2Timothy1:18 May the Lord grant him his mercy on that Day! And you know very well how much he did for me in Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 As for you
2Timothy2:2 Take the teachings that you heard me proclaim in the presence of many witnesses
2Timothy2:3 Take your part in suffering
2Timothy2:4 A soldier on active duty wants to please his commanding officer and so does not get mixed up in the affairs of civilian life.
2Timothy2:5 An athlete who runs in a race cannot win the prize unless he obeys the rules.
2Timothy2:6 The farmer who has done the hard work should have the first share of the harvest.
2Timothy2:7 Think about what I am saying
2Timothy2:8 Remember Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:9 Because I preach the Good News
2Timothy2:10 and so I endure everything for the sake of God's chosen people
2Timothy2:11 This is a true saying: “If we have died with him
2Timothy2:12 If we continue to endure
2Timothy2:13 If we are not faithful
2Timothy2:14 Remind your people of this
2Timothy2:15 Do your best to win full approval in God's sight
2Timothy2:16 Keep away from profane and foolish discussions
2Timothy2:17 Such teaching is like an open sore that eats away the flesh. Two men who have taught such things are Hymenaeus and Philetus.
2Timothy2:18 They have left the way of truth and are upsetting the faith of some believers by saying that our resurrection has already taken place.
2Timothy2:19 But the solid foundation that God has laid cannot be shaken; and on it are written these words: “The Lord knows those who are his” and “Those who say that they belong to the Lord must turn away from wrongdoing.”
2Timothy2:20 In a large house there are dishes and bowls of all kinds: some are made of silver and gold
2Timothy2:21 Those who make themselves clean from all those evil things
2Timothy2:22 Avoid the passions of youth
2Timothy2:23 But keep away from foolish and ignorant arguments; you know that they end up in quarrels.
2Timothy2:24 As the Lord's servant
2Timothy2:25 who is gentle as you correct your opponents
2Timothy2:26 And then they will come to their senses and escape from the trap of the Devil
2Timothy3:1 Remember that there will be difficult times in the last days.
2Timothy3:2 People will be selfish
2Timothy3:3 they will be unkind
2Timothy3:4 they will be treacherous
2Timothy3:5 they will hold to the outward form of our religion
2Timothy3:6 Some of them go into people's houses and gain control over weak women who are burdened by the guilt of their sins and driven by all kinds of desires
2Timothy3:7 women who are always trying to learn but who can never come to know the truth.
2Timothy3:8 As Jannes and Jambres were opposed to Moses
2Timothy3:9 But they will not get very far
2Timothy3:10 But you have followed my teaching
2Timothy3:11 my persecutions
2Timothy3:12 Everyone who wants to live a godly life in union with Christ Jesus will be persecuted;
2Timothy3:13 and evil persons and impostors will keep on going from bad to worse
2Timothy3:14 But as for you
2Timothy3:15 and you remember that ever since you were a child
2Timothy3:16 All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching the truth
2Timothy3:17 so that the person who serves God may be fully qualified and equipped to do every kind of good deed.
2Timothy4:1 In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus
2Timothy4:2 to preach the message
2Timothy4:3 The time will come when people will not listen to sound doctrine
2Timothy4:4 They will turn away from listening to the truth and give their attention to legends.
2Timothy4:5 But you must keep control of yourself in all circumstances; endure suffering
2Timothy4:6 As for me
2Timothy4:7 I have done my best in the race
2Timothy4:8 And now there is waiting for me the victory prize of being put right with God
2Timothy4:9 Do your best to come to me soon.
2Timothy4:10 Demas fell in love with this present world and has deserted me
2Timothy4:11 Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you
2Timothy4:12 I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
2Timothy4:13 When you come
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the metalworker did me great harm; the Lord will reward him according to what he has done.
2Timothy4:15 Be on your guard against him yourself
2Timothy4:16 No one stood by me the first time I defended myself; all deserted me. May God not count it against them!
2Timothy4:17 But the Lord stayed with me and gave me strength
2Timothy4:18 And the Lord will rescue me from all evil and take me safely into his heavenly Kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever! Amen.
2Timothy4:19 I send greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and to the family of Onesiphorus.
2Timothy4:20 Erastus stayed in Corinth
2Timothy4:21 Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus
2Timothy4:22 The Lord be with your spirit. God's grace be with you all.
Titus1:1 From Paul
Titus1:2 which is based on the hope for eternal life. God
Titus1:3 and at the right time he revealed it in his message. This was entrusted to me
Titus1:4 I write to Titus
Titus1:5 I left you in Crete
Titus1:6 an elder must be without fault; he must have only one wife
Titus1:7 For since a church leader is in charge of God's work
Titus1:8 He must be hospitable and love what is good. He must be self-controlled
Titus1:9 He must hold firmly to the message which can be trusted and which agrees with the doctrine. In this way he will be able to encourage others with the true teaching and also to show the error of those who are opposed to it.
Titus1:10 For there are many
Titus1:11 It is necessary to stop their talk
Titus1:12 It was a Cretan himself
Titus1:13 
Titus1:14 and no longer hold on to Jewish legends and to human commandments which come from people who have rejected the truth.
Titus1:15 Everything is pure to those who are themselves pure; but nothing is pure to those who are defiled and unbelieving
Titus1:16 They claim that they know God
Titus2:1 But you must teach what agrees with sound doctrine.
Titus2:2 Instruct the older men to be sober
Titus2:3 In the same way instruct the older women to behave as women should who live a holy life. They must not be slanderers or slaves to wine. They must teach what is good
Titus2:4 in order to train the younger women to love their husbands and children
Titus2:5 to be self-controlled and pure
Titus2:6 In the same way urge the young men to be self-controlled.
Titus2:7 In all things you yourself must be an example of good behavior. Be sincere and serious in your teaching.
Titus2:8 Use sound words that cannot be criticized
Titus2:9 Slaves are to submit themselves to their masters and please them in all things. They must not talk back to them
Titus2:10 or steal from them. Instead
Titus2:11 For God has revealed his grace for the salvation of all people.
Titus2:12 That grace instructs us to give up ungodly living and worldly passions
Titus2:13 as we wait for the blessed Day we hope for
Titus2:14 He gave himself for us
Titus2:15 Teach these things and use your full authority as you encourage and rebuke your hearers. Let none of them look down on you.
Titus3:1 Remind your people to submit to rulers and authorities
Titus3:2 Tell them not to speak evil of anyone
Titus3:3 For we ourselves were once foolish

Hebrews9:6 This is how those things have been arranged. The priests go into the outer tent every day to perform their duties

Hebrews11:1 To have faith is to be sure of the things we hope for
Hebrews11:2 It was by their faith that people of ancient times won God's approval.
Hebrews11:3 It is by faith that we understand that the universe was created by God's word
Hebrews11:4 It was faith that made Abel offer to God a better sacrifice than Cain's. Through his faith he won God's approval as a righteous man
Hebrews11:5 It was faith that kept Enoch from dying. Instead
Hebrews11:6 No one can please God without faith
Hebrews11:7 It was faith that made Noah hear God's warnings about things in the future that he could not see. He obeyed God and built a boat in which he and his family were saved. As a result
Hebrews11:8 It was faith that made Abraham obey when God called him to go out to a country which God had promised to give him. He left his own country without knowing where he was going.
Hebrews11:9 By faith he lived as a foreigner in the country that God had promised him. He lived in tents
Hebrews11:10 For Abraham was waiting for the city which God has designed and built
Hebrews11:11 It was faith that made Abraham able to become a father
Hebrews11:12 Though Abraham was practically dead
Hebrews11:13 It was in faith that all these persons died. They did not receive the things God had promised
Hebrews11:14 Those who say such things make it clear that they are looking for a country of their own.
Hebrews11:15 They did not keep thinking about the country they had left; if they had
Hebrews11:16 Instead

Hebrews11:20 It was faith that made Isaac promise blessings for the future to Jacob and Esau.
Hebrews11:21 It was faith that made Jacob bless each of the sons of Joseph just before he died. He leaned on the top of his walking stick and worshiped God.
Hebrews11:22 It was faith that made Joseph
Hebrews11:23 It was faith that made the parents of Moses hide him for three months after he was born. They saw that he was a beautiful child
Hebrews11:24 It was faith that made Moses
Hebrews11:25 He preferred to suffer with God's people rather than to enjoy sin for a little while.
Hebrews11:26 He reckoned that to suffer scorn for the Messiah was worth far more than all the treasures of Egypt
Hebrews11:27 It was faith that made Moses leave Egypt without being afraid of the king's anger. As though he saw the invisible God
Hebrews11:28 It was faith that made him establish the Passover and order the blood to be sprinkled on the doors
Hebrews11:29 It was faith that made the Israelites able to cross the Red Sea as if on dry land; when the Egyptians tried to do it
Hebrews11:30 It was faith that made the walls of Jericho fall down after the Israelites had marched around them for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 It was faith that kept the prostitute Rahab from being killed with those who disobeyed God
Hebrews11:32 Should I go on? There isn't enough time for me to speak of Gideon

Hebrews11:34 put out fierce fires
Hebrews11:35 Through faith women received their dead relatives raised back to life. Others
Hebrews11:36 Some were mocked and whipped
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 The world was not good enough for them! They wandered like refugees in the deserts and hills
Hebrews11:39 What a record all of these have won by their faith! Yet they did not receive what God had promised
Hebrews11:40 because God had decided on an even better plan for us. His purpose was that only in company with us would they be made perfect.

James3:5 So it is with the tongue: small as it is
James3:6 And the tongue is like a fire. It is a world of wrong
James3:7 We humans are able to tame and have tamed all other creatures—wild animals and birds
James3:8 But no one has ever been able to tame the tongue. It is evil and uncontrollable
James3:9 We use it to give thanks to our Lord and Father and also to curse other people
James3:10 Words of thanksgiving and cursing pour out from the same mouth. My friends
James3:11 No spring of water pours out sweet water and bitter water from the same opening.

James3:18 And goodness is the harvest that is produced from the seeds the peacemakers plant in peace.

2Peter1:2 May grace and peace be yours in full measure through your knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord.

2Peter1:9 But if you do not have them

2Peter2:14 They want to look for nothing but the chance to commit adultery; their appetite for sin is never satisfied. They lead weak people into a trap. Their hearts are trained to be greedy. They are under God's curse!
2Peter2:15 They have left the straight path and have lost their way; they have followed the path taken by Balaam son of Beor
2Peter2:16 and was rebuked for his sin. His donkey spoke with a human voice and stopped the prophet's insane action.
2Peter2:17 These people are like dried-up springs
2Peter2:18 They make proud and stupid statements
2Peter2:19 They promise them freedom while they themselves are slaves of destructive habits—for we are slaves of anything that has conquered us.
2Peter2:20 If people have escaped from the corrupting forces of the world through their knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ
2Peter2:21 It would have been much better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than to know it and then turn away from the sacred command that was given them.
2Peter2:22 What happened to them shows that the proverbs are true: “A dog goes back to what it has vomited” and “A pig that has been washed goes back to roll in the mud.”
